ClimOO Ontology - PlasticO module: Description back to ToC

The ClimOO Ontology is a domain reference ontology for plastics, (micro-)plastic pollution, and related interrelations with climate change, especially within agricultural systems. The ClimOO Ontology is structured in a suite of nested ontological modules. PlasticO is dedicated to represent polymer molecules, plastic materials, pieces of plastic and plastic objects, along with corresponding life cycles and pollution processes. The other modules: coming soon.

Cross-reference for ClimOO Ontology - PlasticO module classes, object properties and data properties back to ToC

This section provides details for each class and property defined by ClimOO Ontology - PlasticO module.

1-D extentc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PATO_0001708

A size quality inhering in an bearer by virtue of the bearer's extension in one dimension.
has super-classes
size c
has sub-classes
diameter c, height c, length c

2-D extentc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PATO_0001709

A size quality inhering in an bearer by virtue of the bearer's extension in two dimensions.
has super-classes
size c
has sub-classes
area c

2-D shapec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PATO_0002006

A shape that inheres in a 2 dimensional entity, such as a cross section or projection of a 3 dimensional entity.
has super-classes
shape c
has sub-classes
superelliptic c

3-D extentc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PATO_0001710

A size quality inhering in an bearer by virtue of the bearer's extension in three dimensions.
has super-classes
size c
has sub-classes
volume c

3-D shapec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PATO_0002266

A shape that inheres in a 3 dimensional entity.
has super-classes
shape c
has sub-classes
convex 3-D shape c

action specificationc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000007

A directive information entity that describes an action the bearer will take.
Source
OBI Plan and Planned Process branch
Term status
http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000122
Example 
Pour the contents of flask 1 into flask 2
has super-classes
directive information entity c

aerosolc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0020115

An aerosol is a disconnected material entity aggregate which: 1. consists of some portion of matter whose aggregate state quality concretizes gaseous and 2. consists of some portion of matter whose aggregate state quality concretises solid or liquid (TODO: express that this portion is also a disconnected material entity aggregate)
has super-classes
disconnected material entity aggregate c

amorphousc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PATO_0001332

A morphology quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's lack of distinct morphology.
has super-classes
morphology c

amorphous polyethylene terephthalatec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0050020

polyethylene terephthalate that is characterized by a non-crystalline structure
has super-classes
polyethylene terephthalate c

areac back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PATO_0001323

A 2-D extent quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's two dimensional extent.
has super-classes
2-D extent c

atactic polypropylenec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0050012

polypropylene in which the methyl groups are randomly distributed along the polymer chain
has super-classes
polypropylene c

atomic force microscopec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0000525

A microscope that maps the surface topography of materials at the nanoscale by scanning it with a mechanical probe.
has super-classes
microscope c

bio-based plastic manufacturing processc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000099

Reference to fossil and nonfossil feedstocks, such asrenewable feedstock, fossil fuel feedstock, biomass feedstock, related roles, and specific types, such as petroleum and biomasses, can be added as needed.
A plastic manufacturing process that has some bio-based plastic polymer molecules as the main input and some portion of bio-based plastic material as the main output.
is equivalent to
plastic manufacturing process c and (has primary output op some portion of bio-based plastic material c)
has super-classes
plastic manufacturing process c

bio-based plastic objectc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000098

A plastic object that is primarily made of bio-based plastic.
has super-classes

bio-based polymer moleculec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000100

Reference to specif feedstock and related production processes can be added, as needed.
A synthetic polymer molecule that is derived from some nonfossil feedstock.
has super-classes
synthetic polymer molecule c

biodegradabilityc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0200001

ClimOO Ontology comment: We did not include the PMDco class for biodegradable polymers because PMDco erroneously refers to biodegradability in the portions of material and not to the constituting polymer molecules. We, however, imported PMDco “biodegradability” class and mapped it as equivalent to ClimOO “biodegradability” class. We also include the PMDco “polybutylene succinate” and “polylactic acid” classes, subclasses of and mapped as subclasses of the ClimOO “portion of biodegradable plastic” class. This aims to be an initial mapping till PMDco revises its class. See: https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0050036 https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0050039 https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0050037
biodegradability is a disposition of a material which specifies its capability to be fully microbially converted into inorganic end products (CO₂, CH₄, mineral salts, biomass) within an environmentally non-harmful timescale.
Source
orginal: "[biodegradability] [...] is understood as the microbial conversion of all its organic constituents to carbon dioxide (or carbon dioxide and methane in conditions where oxygen is not present), new microbial biomass and mineral salts, within a timescale short enough not to lead to lasting harm or accumulation in the open environment." European Commission: Directorate-General for Research and Innovation, Biodegradability of plastics in the open environment, Publications Office of the European Union, 2021, https://data.europa.eu/doi/10.2777/690248
is equivalent to
biodegradability c
has super-classes
disposition c

biodegradabilityc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000104

Definitions of plastic properties, such as biodegradability and composability, and related processes, currently covered in the ClimOO Ontology, refer to existing policy standards. This aims to stress the significance of such regulations concerning defining such entities and, especially, the consequences in terms of pollution and sustainability interventions. It is noteworthy to mention that such standards present limitations. See, for instance, the work of Rayns et al. (2021), Steiner et al. (2022, 2024), concerning plastic biodegradability. We envision implementing such definitions in accordance with regulation revisions and novel research findings.
A disposition that is realised in a biodegradation process.
has super-classes
disposition c
has sub-classes
compostability c

biodegradable plastic objectc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000108

A plastic object that is primarily made of biodegradable plastic.
has super-classes
has sub-classes
compostable plastic object c

biodegradable polymer moleculec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000109

References to degradable polymers and degradability can be added as needed.
A polymer molecule that bears a disposition to biodegradation (biodegradability).
has super-classes
polymer molecule c

biodegradable synthetic plastic manufacturing processc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000111

A plastic manufacturing process that has some biodegradable synthetic polymer molecules as the main input and some portion of biodegradable plastic as the main output.
is equivalent to
plastic manufacturing process c and (has primary output op some portion of synthetic biodegradable plastic material c)
has super-classes
plastic manufacturing process c

biodegradation processc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000263

Definitions of plastic properties, such as biodegradability and composability, and related processes, currently covered in the ClimOO Ontology, refer to existing policy standards. This aims to stress the significance of such regulations concerning defining such entities and, especially, the consequences in terms of pollution and sustainability interventions. It is noteworthy to mention that such standards present limitations. See, for instance, the work of Rayns et al. (2021), Steiner et al. (2022, 2024), concerning plastic biodegradability. We envision implementing such definitions in accordance with regulation revisions and novel research findings.
A process driven by biological entities that changes the physical and chemical qualities of some molecular entities, resulting in a decrease in their molecular weight.
has super-classes
biological process c
has sub-classes
composting process c, polymer biodegradation process c

biodegradation process of a piece of sythetic plasticc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000112

A biodegradation of synthetic plastic that applies to some polymer molecules constituting some portion of plastic that composes some piece of biodegradable synthetic plastic.
is equivalent to
biodegradation process of synthetic plastic c and (has input op some (biodegradable polymer molecule c and synthetic polymer molecule c) and (continuant part of at all times op some portion of synthetic biodegradable plastic material c and (continuant part of at all times op some piece of biodegradable synthetic plastic c)))
has super-classes
biodegradation process of synthetic plastic c

biodegradation process of plasticc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000264

To date, the ClimOO ontology does not restrict such classes to solely refer to wasted plastics. Implementations may extend the current representation in such a direction, if needed. The only exception is the “plastic composting process” class, given that the aimed specific focus on composting of waste plastics.
a polymer biodegradation that applies to some biodegradable polymer molecules constituting a portion of biodegradable plastic.
is equivalent to
polymer biodegradation process c and (has input op some biodegradable polymer molecule c and (continuant part of at all times op some portion of biodegradable plastic material c))
has super-classes
plastic degradation process c
has sub-classes
biodegradation process of synthetic plastic c

biodegradation process of synthetic plasticc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000113

A biodegradation of plastic that applies to some synthetic polymer molecules, constituting some portion of synthetic plastic (portion of biodegradable synthetic plastic). Packaging applications: 90% of the total polymer molecular weight must be degraded till mineralization into H2O, CO2, and mineral salts, in the presence of oxygen, and methane, H2O, CO2, and mineral salts, in the absence of oxygen, within the first 6 months (according to EU EN 13432, standardized laboratory test conditions). (See EU EN 13432, UNI EN ISO 20200, and Regulation - 2023/2055 - EN - EUR-Lex).
is equivalent to
synthetic plastic degradation process c and (has input op some (biodegradable polymer molecule c and synthetic polymer molecule c) and (continuant part of at all times op some portion of synthetic biodegradable plastic material c))
has super-classes
biodegradation process of plastic c
has sub-classes
biodegradation process of a piece of sythetic plastic c, plastic composting process c

biological processc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0008150

A biological process is the execution of a genetically-encoded biological module or program. It consists of all the steps required to achieve the specific biological objective of the module. A biological process is accomplished by a particular set of molecular functions carried out by specific gene products (or macromolecular complexes), often in a highly regulated manner and in a particular temporal sequence.
ClimOO Ontology: we excluded the parthood axiom "BFO_0000051 (has part) some GO_0003674 (gene product or complex activity) as beyond ClimOO Ontology purposes. http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0003674
has super-classes
process c
has sub-classes
biodegradation process c
is disjoint with
completely executed planned process c

biological rolec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CHEBI_24432

A role played by the molecular entity or part thereof within a biological context.
has super-classes
role c
has sub-classes
physiological role c, poison c, xenobiotic c

biomineralization process of piece of synthetic biodegradable plasticc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000272

A synthetic plastic biomineralization process that applies to some biodegradable synthetic polymer molecules constituting some potions of biodegradable synthetic plastic composing some piece of biodegradable synthetic plastic.
is equivalent to
synthetic plastic biomineralization process c and (has input op some (biodegradable polymer molecule c and synthetic polymer molecule c) and (continuant part of at all times op some portion of synthetic biodegradable plastic material c and (continuant part of at all times op some piece of biodegradable synthetic plastic c)))
has super-classes
synthetic plastic biomineralization process c

bisphenol a epoxyc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0050024

epoxy resin that is formulated using bisphenol a to enhance its mechanical properties
has super-classes
epoxy resin c

Blackc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.ontologyrepository.com/CommonCoreOntologies/Black

A Color that lacks any hues as parts.
has super-classes
Color c

Bluec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.ontologyrepository.com/CommonCoreOntologies/Blue

A Color that is between Cyan and Violet with a wavelength in the visible spectrum typically between 450 to 490 nanometers.
has super-classes
Color c

Brownc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.ontologyrepository.com/CommonCoreOntologies/Brown

A Color that consists of dark orange and red, and of very low intensity.
has super-classes
Color c

chemical entityc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CHEBI_24431

A chemical entity is a physical entity of interest in chemistry including molecular entities, parts thereof, and chemical substances.
has super-classes
material entity c
has sub-classes
chemical substance c, molecular entity c

chemical rolec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CHEBI_51086

A role played by the molecular entity or part thereof within a chemical context.
has super-classes
role c
has sub-classes
environmental contaminant c

chemical substancec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CHEBI_59999

A chemical substance is a portion of matter of constant composition, composed of molecular entities of the same type or of different types.
Frequently used for fluidic portions of matter and/or in the context of chemical reactions. Boundary to material not always sharp.
has super-classes
chemical entity c, portion of matter c
has sub-classes
mixture c, pure substance c

chromatography systemc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0000557

A device used for separating mixtures into individual components using a chromatographic column and a mobile phase.
has super-classes
device c
has sub-classes
gas chromatography system c, gel permeation chromatography system c, high performance liquid chromatography system c, ion exchange chromatography system c, supercritical fluid chromatography system c, thin layer chromatography system c

circularc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PATO_0000411

A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being such that every part of the surface or the circumference is equidistant from the center.
has super-classes
elliptic c

Colorc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.ontologyrepository.com/CommonCoreOntologies/Color

An Optical Property that inheres in a bearer in virtue of that bearer's Color Hue, Color Saturation and Color Brightness.
has super-classes
Optical Property c
has sub-classes
Black c, Blue c, Brown c, Green c, Grey c, Orange c, Red c, White c, Yellow c, transparent c

combustibilityc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000243

A disposition that realizes in a process of combustion.
has super-classes
disposition c

completely executed planned processc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/COB_0000035

ClimOO Ontology comment: Currently, we focus the following ClimOO classes on completely executed processes. Implementations can enhance the model to account for processes that might fail.
has super-classes
planned process c
has sub-classes
disposal process of a piece of synthetic polymer-based material c, material processing c, plastic pollution prevention process c, usage process of some piece of synthetic polymer-based material c
is in domain of
achieves_planned_objective op, has specified input op, has specified output op
is in range of
is specified input of op, is specified output of op, objective_achieved_by op
is disjoint with
failed planned process c, biological process c

complex systemc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/sco#SCO_0000015

A system whose system components interact dynamically and nonlinearly, causing the emergence of a collective nontrivial and self-organized behavior. No internal/external centralized controls are required.
Source
Ubbiali, G. A., Borghini, A., & Lange, M. C. (2024). Ontologies for Sustainability: Theoretical Challenges. https://doi.org/10.31219/osf.io/z8uqr
is equivalent to
system c and (has quality op some emergence c or self-organization c)
has super-classes
system c
is disjoint with
complex system environment c

complex system agentc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/sco#SCO_0000034

A complex system component that is an agent in an interaction among complex system components or in an interaction between complex system components and complex system environment components.
is equivalent to
complex system component c and (agent in at time t op some interaction between complex system components and complex system environment components c or interaction among complex system components c) and (agent in at time t op only interaction between complex system components and complex system environment components c or interaction among complex system components c)
has super-classes
system agent c
is disjoint with
complex system environment agent c

complex system boundaryc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/sco#SCO_0000031

A system boundary of a complex system.
is equivalent to
system boundary c and (proper continuant part of at all times op some complex system c)
has super-classes
system boundary c

complex system componentc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/sco#SCO_0000028

A system component that belongs to a complex system.
is equivalent to
system component c and (continuant part of op some complex system c)
has super-classes
system component c
has sub-classes
indicator c, stakeholder c
is disjoint with
complex system environment component c

complex system developmentc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/sco#SCO_0000010

The system development of a complex system.
has super-classes
system development c
is disjoint with
complex system history c

complex system environmentc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/sco#SCO_0000012

A system environment that surrounds and is distinct and disjoint from a complex system.
has super-classes
system environment c
is disjoint with
complex system c

complex system environment agentc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/sco#SCO_0000033

A complex system environment component that is an agent in an interaction among complex system environment components or in an interaction between complex system components and complex system environment components.
is equivalent to
complex system environment component c and (agent in at time t op some interaction between complex system components and complex system environment components c or interaction among complex system environment components c) and (agent in at time t op only interaction between complex system components and complex system environment components c or interaction among complex system environment components c)
has super-classes
system environnement agent c
is disjoint with
complex system agent c

complex system environment componentc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/sco#SCO_0000026

A system evironment component that belongs to the system environment of a complex system.
is equivalent to
system environment component c and (continuant part of op some complex system environment c)
has super-classes
system environment component c
is disjoint with
complex system component c

complex system historyc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/sco#SCO_0000023

The system history of a complex system.
is equivalent to
system history c and (history of op some complex system c) and (history of op only complex system c)
has super-classes
system history c
is disjoint with
complex system development c

complex system propertyc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/sco#SCO_0000041

Do not use this class directly. This class is constructed as a grouping for complex system properties, such as emergence and self-organization, in order to facilitate class retrieval and visualization.
propriété de système complexe
has super-classes
specifically dependent continuant c
has sub-classes
emergence c, self-organization c

complex system spatial regionc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/sco#SCO_0000032

A system spatial region of a complex system.
has super-classes
system spatial region c

compostabilityc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000115

A biodegradability disposition whose realization leads an environmental material to biodegrade while participating in a composting process.
is equivalent to
biodegradability c and (has realization op only composting process c)
has super-classes
biodegradability c

compostable plastic objectc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000116

A plastic object that is primarily made of compostable plastic.
has super-classes
biodegradable plastic object c

composting processc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000119

Definitions of plastic properties, such as biodegradability and composability, and related processes, currently covered in the ClimOO Ontology, refer to existing policy standards. This aims to stress the significance of such regulations concerning defining such entities and, especially, the consequences in terms of pollution and sustainability interventions. It is noteworthy to mention that such standards present limitations. See, for instance, the work of Rayns et al. (2021), Steiner et al. (2022, 2024), concerning plastic biodegradability. We envision implementing such definitions in accordance with regulation revisions and novel research findings.
A biodegradation process that occurs in the presence of oxygen, has, as main input, some organic waste, and has, a main output, some compost. (See EU EN 13432, UNI EN ISO 20200, and Regulation - 2023/2055 - EN - EUR-Lex).
is equivalent to
biodegradation process c and (realizes op some compostability c)
has super-classes
biodegradation process c

connected material entity aggregatec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0000890

An object aggregate that is a mereological sum of separate material entities, which adhere to one another through chemical bonds or physical junctions that go beyond gravity.
Example 
the atoms of a molecule, the molecules forming the membrane of a cell, the epidermis in a human body
has super-classes
object aggregate c
has sub-classes
foam c

continuantc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000002

An entity that exists in full at any time in which it exists at all, persists through time while maintaining its identity and has no temporal parts.
(Elucidation) A continuant is an entity that persists, endures, or continues to exist through time while maintaining its identity
Example 
A human being; a tennis ball; a cave; a region of space; someone's temperature
has super-classes
entity c
has sub-classes
generically dependent continuant c, independent continuant c, specifically dependent continuant c
is in domain of
continuant part of op, continuant part of at all times op, has continuant part op, has continuant part at all times op, has proper continuant part at all times op, has proper continuant part at some time op, proper continuant part of at all times op, proper continuant part of at some time op
is in range of
continuant part of op, continuant part of at all times op, has continuant part op, has continuant part at all times op, has proper continuant part at all times op, has proper continuant part at some time op, proper continuant part of at all times op, proper continuant part of at some time op
is disjoint with
occurrent c

continuant fiat boundaryc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000140

(Elucidation) A continuant fiat boundary b is an immaterial entity that is of zero, one or two dimensions & such that there is no time t when b has a spatial region as continuant part & whose location is determined in relation to some material entity
Example 
As for fiat point, fiat line, fiat surface
has super-classes
immaterial entity c
has sub-classes
fiat line c, fiat point c, fiat surface c
is disjoint with
spatial region c, site c

convex 3-D shapec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PATO_0002007

A complete three dimensional shape in which for every line connecting pair of points on the object is within the object. Or: a shape lacking cavities. Contrast: concave.
ClimOO Ontology: It seems revisions are required for this class. The axiomatization does not seem comply with the BFO model. We envision discussions with subject matter experts.
has super-classes
3-D shape c
has sub-classes
cylindrical c, spheroid c

copolymerc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CHEBI_60804

A polymer, composed of copolymer macromolecules.
has super-classes
polymer c

copolymer macromoleculec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CHEBI_53310

A macromolecule derived from two or more species of monomer.
has super-classes
macromolecule c

crystalline polyethylene terephthalatec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0050021

polyethylene terephthalate that is distinguished by its ordered, crystalline structure
has super-classes
polyethylene terephthalate c

curingc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0010034

The toughening or hardening of a polymer material by cross-linking of polymer chains.
has super-classes
process c
has sub-classes
thermosetting polymer curing process c

cylindricalc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PATO_0001873

A convex 3-D shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's exhibiting a consistently-sized round cross section.
has super-classes
convex 3-D shape c
has sub-classes
subcylindrical c

data itemc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000027

An information content entity that is intended to be a truthful statement about something (modulo, e.g., measurement precision or other systematic errors) and is constructed/acquired by a method which reliably tends to produce (approximately) truthful statements.
Term status
http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000125
Example 
Data items include counts of things, analyte concentrations, and statistical summaries.
has super-classes
information content entity c
has sub-classes
measurement datum c, setting datum c

datum labelc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000009

A label is a symbol that is part of some other datum and is used to either partially define the denotation of that datum or to provide a means for identifying the datum as a member of the set of data with the same label
Term status
http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000123
has super-classes
information content entity c
has sub-classes
measurement unit label c

degradation process of a piece of synthetic plasticc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000270

A plastic degradation process applying to polymer molecules constituting some portion of synthetic plastic that composes some piece of synthetic plastic.
is equivalent to
synthetic plastic degradation process c and (has input op some synthetic polymer molecule c and (continuant part of at all times op some portion of synthetic plastic material c and (continuant part of at all times op some piece of synthetic plastic c)))
has super-classes
degradation process of a piece of synthetic polymer-based material c

degradation process of a piece of synthetic polymer-based materialc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000275

A degradation process of a piece of synthetic polymer-based material that leads some synthetic polymer molecules constituting some portion of synthetic polymer-bsed material composing some piece of syntehtic polymer-based material to vary their physical and chemical qualities due to interactions with factors such as heat, light, or chemical entities, resulting in a decrease in their molecular weight.
is equivalent to
synthetic polymer-based material degradation process c and (has input op some synthetic polymer molecule c and (continuant part of at all times op some portion of synthetic polymer-based material c and (continuant part of at all times op some piece of polymer-based synthetic material c)))
has super-classes
synthetic polymer-based material degradation process c
has sub-classes
degradation process of a piece of synthetic plastic c, plastic debris degradation process c

Descriptive Information Content Entityc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.ontologyrepository.com/CommonCoreOntologies/DescriptiveInformationContentEntity

An Information Content Entity that consists of a set of propositions that describe some Entity.
has super-classes
Information Content Entity c
has sub-classes
Measurement Information Content Entity c, Measurement Unit c
is in domain of
describes op
is in range of
described by op
is disjoint with
Directive Information Content Entity c

devicec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0000602

A device is an object that is designed to perform a specific function or task involving measurement, manipulation, processing, or analysis.
has super-classes
object c
has sub-classes
chromatography system c, mass spectrometer c, microscope c

device rolec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0000603

Rolle, die ein Geräte inne haben kann.
has super-classes
role c

diameterc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PATO_0001334

A length quality which is equal to the length of any straight line segment that passes through the center of a circle and whose endpoints are on the circular boundary.
has super-classes
1-D extent c

Directive Information Content Entityc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.ontologyrepository.com/CommonCoreOntologies/DirectiveInformationContentEntity

An Information Content Entity that consists of a set of propositions or images (as in the case of a blueprint) that prescribe some Entity.
is equivalent to
Information Content Entity c and (prescribes op some entity c)
has super-classes
Information Content Entity c
is in domain of
prescribes op
is in range of
prescribed by op
is disjoint with
Descriptive Information Content Entity c

directive information entityc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000033

An information content entity whose concretizations indicate to their bearer how to realize them in a process.
Term status
http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000122
is equivalent to
Directive Information Content Entity c
has super-classes
information content entity c
has sub-classes
action specification c, objective specification c, plan specification c, specification datum c

disconnected material entity aggregatec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0000891

An object aggregate that is a mereological sum of scattered (i.e. spatially separated) material entities, which do not adhere to one another through chemical bonds or physical junctions but, instead, relate to one another merely on grounds of metric proximity. The material entities are separated from one another through space or through other material entities that do not belong to the group.
Example 
a heap of stones, a colony of honeybees, a group of synapses, the trees of a forest, the canopy of a forest, the fish of a shoal, a group of commuters on the subway, the patients in a hospital
has super-classes
object aggregate c
has sub-classes
aerosol c, powder c

disposal process of a piece of synthetic plasticc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000280

A disposal process of a piece of polymer-based material that applies to piece of plastic deemed to be waste, resulting in its removal and demise.
has super-classes
disposal process of a piece of synthetic polymer-based material c
has sub-classes
disposal process of a plastic object c

disposal process of a piece of synthetic polymer-based materialc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000279

Depending on the regulations and standards of reference, recycling may or may not be classified as a method of disposal. Sustainability impacts and implications may diverge equally across methods. Currently, the ClimOO Ontology remains agnostic in those stances, making these two classes not disjoint. Users can specify the model based on their needs and according to endorsed standards. See, for instance, https://www.iso.org/standard/77957.html
A planned process that applies to piece of synthetic polymer-based material deemed to be waste, resulting in its removal and demise.
has super-classes
completely executed planned process c
has sub-classes
disposal process of a piece of synthetic plastic c

disposal process of a plastic objectc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000281

A disposal process of a piece of plastic' that applies to some plastic object deemed to be waste, resulting in its removal and demise.
has super-classes
disposal process of a piece of synthetic plastic c

dispositionc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000016

(Elucidation) A disposition b is a realizable entity such that if b ceases to exist then its bearer is physically changed & b's realization occurs when and because this bearer is in some special physical circumstances & this realization occurs in virtue of the bearer's physical make-up
Example 
An atom of element X has the disposition to decay to an atom of element Y; the cell wall is disposed to transport cellular material through endocytosis and exocytosis; certain people have a predisposition to colon cancer; children are innately disposed to categorize objects in certain ways
has super-classes
realizable entity c
has sub-classes
Electromagnetic Radiation Property c, biodegradability c, biodegradability c, combustibility c, disposition to motion in the environment c, disposition to polymerize c, elasticity c, environmental persistence c, function c, perspective c, recyclability c, resilience c, risk c, thermoplasticity c, thermosetting disposition c, vulnerability c, water solubility c
is in domain of
has material basis op, has material basis at all times op
is in range of
has disposition op, material basis of op, material basis of at all times op
is disjoint with
role c

disposition to motion in the environmentc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000241

Reference to corresponding motion processes can be added as needed.
A disposition that realizes in a bearer when participating in a process of motion that occurs in an environmental system/environmental system environment.
has super-classes
disposition c

disposition to polymerizec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000123

A disposition that realizes in a polymerization process.
Source
Compendium of polymer terminology and nomenclature: IUPAC recommendations, 2008. (2009). Choice (Middletown), 47(01), 47-0284-47–0284. Vert, M., Doi, Y., Hellwich, K.-H., Hess, M., Hodge, P., Kubisa, P., Rinaudo, M., & Schué, F. (2012). Terminology for biorelated polymers and applications (IUPAC Recommendations 2012). Pure Appl. Chem., 84(2), 377–410.
has super-classes
disposition c

elasticityc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000124

References to processes realizing this disposition can be added as needed.
A disposition whose realization leads the bearer to recover its size and shape after deformation.
has super-classes
disposition c
is disjoint with
thermoplasticity c, thermosetting disposition c

elastomerc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0050030

polymer that exhibits elasticity by returning to its original shape after deformation
is equivalent to
portion of elastomer material c
has super-classes
polymer c
has sub-classes
natural rubber c, synthetic rubber c

elastomer moleculec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000125

A polymer molecule that is part of some portion of polymer-based material having a disposition to be elastic (elasticity).
is equivalent to
polymer molecule c and (continuant part of at all times op some portion of polymer-based material c and (has disposition op some elasticity c))
has super-classes
polymer molecule c
has sub-classes
thermoplastic elastomer molecule c

Electromagnetic Radiation Propertyc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.ontologyrepository.com/CommonCoreOntologies/ElectromagneticRadiationProperty

A Disposition that inheres in an bearer in virtue of how that bearer interacts with electromagnetic radiation.
has super-classes
disposition c
has sub-classes
Optical Property c

electron microscopec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0000624

A microscope that uses a beam of electrons to create high-resolution images of a sample's surface or structure.
Source
“Electron microscope.” Merriam-Webster.com Dictionary, Merriam-Webster, https://www.merriam-webster.com/dictionary/electron%20microscope. Accessed 24 Nov. 2022.
has super-classes
microscope c
has sub-classes
scanning electron microscope c, transmission electron microscope c

ellipticc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PATO_0000947

A spheroid quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being oval with two axes of symmetry, as produced by a conical section.
has super-classes
superelliptic c
has sub-classes
circular c

emergencec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/sco#SCO_0000042

A more extensive representation of emergence is required. A possible starting point may be the work of Calhau et al. (2024). Calhau, R. F., Prince Sales, T., Oliveira, Í., Kokkula, S., Ferreira Pires, L., Cameron, D., Guizzardi, G., & Almeida, J. P. A. (2024). A System Core Ontology for Capability Emergence Modeling. In H. A. Proper, L. Pufahl, D. Karastoyanova, M. Van Sinderen, & J. Moreira (A c. Di), Enterprise Design, Operations, and Computing (Vol. 14367, pp. 3–20). Springer Nature Switzerland. https://doi.org/10.1007/978-3-031-46587-1_1
A quality that inherits in a complex system by virtue of how the complex system behavior and properties at a macroscopic level are nontrivially envisaged from those at a more microscopic one.
has super-classes
complex system property c
is disjoint with
self-organization c

entityc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000001

(Elucidation) An entity is anything that exists or has existed or will exist
Example 
Julius Caesar; the Second World War; your body mass index; Verdi's Requiem
has sub-classes
continuant c, occurrent c
is in domain of
denoted by op, exists at op
is in range of
denotes op, is about op

environmental contaminantc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CHEBI_78298

Any minor or unwanted substance introduced into the environment that can have undesired effects.
has super-classes
chemical role c
has sub-classes
environmental food contaminant c, persistent organic pollutant c

environmental food contaminantc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CHEBI_78299

Any unwanted chemical in food. The term includes agrochemicals and industrial chemicals that may contaminate foodstuffs during their production, transportation or storage.
has super-classes
environmental contaminant c, food component c

environmental persistencec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000126

We envision discussion with subject matter experts to implement the ClimOO Ontology representation of environmental persistence with regard to threshold, especially temporal thresholds.
An environmental disposition whose realization leads an environmental material to hold its qualities within the system (system environment) to which it belongs, for a period of time judged long enough according to some stakeholder perspective (metrics, standards).
has super-classes
disposition c

epoxy resinc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0050023

thermosetting polymer that is a epoxide-functional oligomer curing via ring-opening into crosslinked networks used as precursors to thermosetting polymers
has super-classes
thermosetting polymer c
has sub-classes
bisphenol a epoxy c, novolac epoxy c

ethylene propylene diene monomerc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0050035

synthetic rubber produced from ethylene, propylene, and a diene monomer
has super-classes
synthetic rubber c

exposure processc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.unep.org/sdg/SDGIO_00000014

A process which is causally within or downstream of another process whose direct or indirect effects cause one or more entities to realise one or more of their dispositions.
Consider that when "E died of exposure to P", E died as an outcome of the processes which were initiated by E's participation in P, in which E's dispositions to react to P (or its parts or participants) were realised.
Term status
http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000428
has super-classes
process c

failed planned processc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/COB_0000083

has super-classes
planned process c
is disjoint with
completely executed planned process c

fiat linec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000142

(Elucidation) A fiat line is a one-dimensional continuant fiat boundary that is continuous
Example 
The Equator; all geopolitical boundaries; all lines of latitude and longitude; the median sulcus of your tongue; the line separating the outer surface of the mucosa of the lower lip from the outer surface of the skin of the chin
has super-classes
continuant fiat boundary c
is disjoint with
fiat surface c, fiat point c

fiat object partc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000024

(Elucidation) A fiat object part b is a material entity & such that if b exists then it is continuant part of some object c & demarcated from the remainder of c by one or more fiat surfaces
Example 
The upper and lower lobes of the left lung; the dorsal and ventral surfaces of the body; the Western hemisphere of the Earth; the FMA:regional parts of an intact human body
has super-classes
material entity c

fiat pointc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000147

(Elucidation) A fiat point is a zero-dimensional continuant fiat boundary that consists of a single point
Example 
The geographic North Pole; the quadripoint where the boundaries of Colorado, Utah, New Mexico and Arizona meet; the point of origin of some spatial coordinate system
has super-classes
continuant fiat boundary c
is disjoint with
fiat line c, fiat surface c

fiat surfacec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000146

(Elucidation) A fiat surface is a two-dimensional continuant fiat boundary that is self-connected
Example 
The surface of the Earth; the plane separating the smoking from the non-smoking zone in a restaurant
has super-classes
continuant fiat boundary c
has sub-classes
system boundary c
is disjoint with
fiat line c, fiat point c

fiber shapedc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PATO_0002309

A convex 3-D shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's exhibiting a by virtue of the bearer's exhibiting a consistently sized and approximately round cross-section along its length, which is many times larger than its diameter.
Note that a fiber shaped object may take any circuitous or straight path through space (think of a length of string or rope).
has super-classes
subcylindrical c

film debris particlec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000127

A plastic debris particle that is flexible, thin, and planar.
has super-classes
plastic debris particle c

flexible polyvinyl chloridec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0050015

polyvinyl chloride that has been modified with plasticizers to impart flexibility
has super-classes
polyvinyl chloride c

foamc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0020126

A foam is a connected material entity aggregate that consists of a solid or liquid matrix containing gas-filled or vacuum-filled pores forming cellular structures.
Source
old defintion: A foam is a material entity aggregate that is also a composite material c. The parts of c that have the filler role are vacuum-filled or gas filled pores. The parts of c that have the matrix role consist of a material that is bearer of solid or liquid aggregate state. One pore with its surrounding matrix is called 'cell'. Depending on the interconnectedness of the pores the foam is open- or closed-cell.
has super-classes
connected material entity aggregate c

food componentc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CHEBI_78295

A physiological role played by any substance that is distributed in foodstuffs. It includes materials derived from plants or animals, such as vitamins or minerals, as well as environmental contaminants.
has super-classes
physiological role c
has sub-classes
environmental food contaminant c

fragmentation process of a piece of polymer-based materialc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000273

Definitions of plastic properties, such as biodegradability and composability, and related processes, currently covered in the ClimOO Ontology, refer to existing policy standards. This aims to stress the significance of such regulations concerning defining such entities and, especially, the consequences in terms of pollution and sustainability interventions. It is noteworthy to mention that such standards present limitations. See, for instance, the work of Rayns et al. (2021), Steiner et al. (2022, 2024), concerning plastic biodegradability. We envision implementing such definitions in accordance with regulation revisions and novel research findings.
A process that leads to breaking pieces of polymer-based material down into pieces of a smaller size.
has super-classes
process c
has sub-classes
fragmentation process of a piece of synthetic polymer-based material c, plastic fragmentation process c

fragmentation process of a piece of synthetic polymer-based materialc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000128

Definitions of plastic properties, such as biodegradability and composability, and related processes, currently covered in the ClimOO Ontology, refer to existing policy standards. This aims to stress the significance of such regulations concerning defining such entities and, especially, the consequences in terms of pollution and sustainability interventions. It is noteworthy to mention that such standards present limitations. See, for instance, the work of Rayns et al. (2021), Steiner et al. (2022, 2024), concerning plastic biodegradability. We envision implementing such definitions in accordance with regulation revisions and novel research findings.
A process that leads to breaking down pieces of synthetic polymer-based material into pieces of a smaller size.
has super-classes
fragmentation process of a piece of polymer-based material c
has sub-classes
plastic debris fragmentation process c

functionc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000034

(Elucidation) A function is a disposition that exists in virtue of its bearer's physical make-up & this physical make-up is something the bearer possesses because it came into being either through evolution (in the case of natural biological entities) or through intentional design (in the case of artefacts) in order to realize processes of a certain sort
Example 
The function of a hammer to drive in nails; the function of a heart pacemaker to regulate the beating of a heart through electricity
has super-classes
disposition c
is in domain of
function of op
is in range of
has function op

gas chromatography systemc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0000657

A chromatography system used for separating and analyzing compounds in a gas mixture using a chromatographic column.
has super-classes
chromatography system c
has sub-classes
high temperature gas chromatography system c

gel permeation chromatography systemc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0000659

A chromatography system used for separating and analyzing polymers based on their molecular size using gel permeation chromatography.
has super-classes
chromatography system c

general purpose polystyrenec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0050017

polystyrene that is valued for its clarity and ease of processing
has super-classes
polystyrene c

generically dependent continuantc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000031

b is a generically dependent continuant = Def. b is a continuant that g-depends_on one or more other entities. (axiom label in BFO2 Reference: [074-001])
(Elucidation) A generically dependent continuant is an entity that exists in virtue of the fact that there is at least one of what may be multiple copies which is the content or the pattern that multiple copies would share
Example 
The pdf file on your laptop; the pdf file that is a copy thereof on my laptop; the sequence of this protein molecule; the sequence that is a copy thereof in that protein molecule; the content that is shared by a string of dots and dashes written on a page and the transmitted Morse code signal; the content of a sentence; an engineering blueprint
has super-classes
continuant c
has sub-classes
Information Content Entity c, information content entity c
is in domain of
agent in at time t op, generically depends on op, generically depends on at all times op, is concretized by op, is concretized by at all times op, participates in op, participates in at all times op
is in range of
concretizes op, concretizes at all times op, has agent at time t op, has participant op, has participant at all times op, is carrier of op, is carrier of at all times op
is disjoint with
independent continuant c, specifically dependent continuant c

granule of plastic foamc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ENVO_06105026

A piece of plastic foam which has a near-spherical or granular shape.
These granules are usually white or opaque but can be any color.

has super-classes

polystyrene that is modified with rubber to improve its impact resistance

high performance liquid chromatography systemc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0000784

A chromatography system used for separating, identifying, and quantifying compounds in liquid samples through high performance liquid chromatography.
has super-classes
chromatography system c

high temperature gas chromatography systemc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0000785

A gas chromatography system used for separating and analyzing compounds in a gas mixture at high temperatures using a chromatographic column.
has super-classes
gas chromatography system c

high-density polyethylenec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0050007

polyethylene that is characterized by a linear molecular structure and high density
has super-classes
portion of polyethylene c

historyc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000182

(Elucidation) A history is a process that is the sum of the totality of processes taking place in the spatiotemporal region occupied by the material part of a material entity
Example 
The life of an organism from the beginning to the end of its existence
has super-classes
process c
has sub-classes
system history c
is in domain of
history of op
is in range of
has history op

immaterial entityc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000141

b is an immaterial entity =Def b is an independent continuant which is such that there is no time t when it has a material entity as continuant part
Example 
As for fiat point, fiat line, fiat surface, site
has super-classes
independent continuant c
has sub-classes
continuant fiat boundary c, site c, spatial region c
is disjoint with
material entity c

independent continuantc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000004

b is an independent continuant = Def. b is a continuant which is such that there is no c and no t such that b s-depends_on c at t. (axiom label in BFO2 Reference: [017-002])
b is an independent continuant =Def b is a continuant & there is no c such that b specifically depends on c or b generically depends on c
Example 
An atom; a molecule; an organism; a heart; a chair; the bottom right portion of a human torso; a leg; the interior of your mouth; a spatial region; an orchestra
has super-classes
continuant c
has sub-classes
immaterial entity c, material entity c
is in domain of
has disposition op, has function op, has role op
is disjoint with
specifically dependent continuant c, generically dependent continuant c

indicatorc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/sco#SCO_0000035

SCO indicator classes have been designed referring to the characterization of sustainable development indicators (SDGs indicators) proposed by SDGIO. we aimed to deepen this characterization and establish upper-level classes for SDGIO ones.
A complex system component with an indicator role.
is equivalent to
complex system component c and (has role op some indicator-role c)
has super-classes
complex system component c

indicator valuec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/sco#SCO_0000003

A representational information content entity that generically depends on some indicator and is about, providing insights into, the complex system to which the indicator belongs.
is equivalent to
genid1312
has super-classes
Representational Information Content Entity c

indicator-rolec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/sco#SCO_0000017

To distinguish and account for indicators according to the perspectives of different complex systems (and environment) components, we suggest proceeding following the indications provided under the “according to the perspective” annotation property. In this case, regarding the subclasses to create, we recommend establishing axioms restricting to this class.
A role whose realization leads the complex system component to be recognized as a carrier of an indicator value, by some complex system component or complex system environment component according to its/their perspective.
has super-classes
role c

information content entityc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000030

A generically dependent continuant that is about some thing.
ClimOO Ontology: We aligned CCO and IAO classes according to SuMSO model.
Source
OBI_0000142
Term status
http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000122
Example 
Examples of information content entites include journal articles, data, graphical layouts, and graphs.
is equivalent to
Information Content Entity c
has super-classes
generically dependent continuant c
has sub-classes
data item c, datum label c, directive information entity c
is in domain of
denotes op, is about op
is in range of
denoted by op

Information Content Entityc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.ontologyrepository.com/CommonCoreOntologies/InformationContentEntity

SCO developer comment: Currently, SCO-B reuses the representation of information that belongs to IAO/CCO. Nevertheless, debates are ongoing on whether information may be better represented through a type-token relationship within a multi-level type hierarchy. See the paper cited at the end of the comment. We envision discussions with subject matter experts from both BFO and UFO communities regarding this point. Weigand, H. (2025). Generic Ontological Dependence⋆. https://www.utwente.nl/en/eemcs/vmbo2025/papers/vmbo-2025-paper-4-2.pdf
is equivalent to
generically dependent continuant c and (is about op some entity c)
has super-classes
generically dependent continuant c
has sub-classes
Descriptive Information Content Entity c, Directive Information Content Entity c, Representational Information Content Entity c, perspective information content entity c, worth information content entity c
is in domain of
is about op
is in range of
is subject of op

inorganic or hybrid polymer moleculec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000129

Specifications of this class can be added as needed.
A polymer molecule that is composed of at least some inorganic monomers.
has super-classes
polymer molecule c

input specificationc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0000014

A parameter specification that prescribes a data item required as input for the execution of a workflow function.
has super-classes
parameter specification c

interactionc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/sco#SCO_0000001

In the original definition, INO developers define this class with respect to interactors involved in the interaction process. INO includes the classes “interactor” and “interactor role” (see SeeAlso note). Here, we generalize this definition, excluding reference to interactors. In the context of SCO, we aim to provide users with an upper-level class that generally points to the involvement of material entities in an interaction process mutually influencing each other. We suggest not using this class directly but, instead, extending SCO-provided subclasses as needed. We wish users to classify interactions according to whether they occur among system components, among system environment components, or between system and system environment components. Original definition: “Interaction is a processual entity that has two or more participants (i.e., interactors) that have an effect upon one another.”
A process that has at least two independent continuants (no spatial regions) as participants that have an effect upon one another.
is equivalent to
genid1287
has super-classes
process c
has sub-classes
interaction among system components c, interaction among system environment components c, interaction between system components and system environment components c

interaction among complex system componentsc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/sco#SCO_0000022

An interaction that occurs among at least two system components of a complex system.
has super-classes
interaction among system components c
is disjoint with
interaction between complex system components and complex system environment components c, interaction among complex system environment components c

interaction among complex system environment componentsc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/sco#SCO_0000021

An interaction that occurs among at least two complex system environment components.
has super-classes
interaction among system environment components c
is disjoint with
interaction between complex system components and complex system environment components c, interaction among complex system components c

interaction among system componentsc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/sco#SCO_0000007

An interaction that occurs among at least two system components.
has super-classes
interaction c
has sub-classes
interaction among complex system components c
is disjoint with
interaction among system environment components c, interaction between system components and system environment components c

interaction among system environment componentsc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/sco#SCO_0000008

An interaction that occurs among at least two system environment components.
has super-classes
interaction c
has sub-classes
interaction among complex system environment components c
is disjoint with
interaction among system components c, interaction between system components and system environment components c

interaction between complex system components and complex system environment componentsc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/sco#SCO_0000020

An interaction that occurs between at least one complex system component and one complex system environment component where the latter belongs to the complex system environment of the complex system to which the complex system component belongs.
has super-classes
interaction between system components and system environment components c
is disjoint with
interaction among complex system environment components c, interaction among complex system components c

interaction between system components and system environment componentsc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/sco#SCO_0000009

An interaction that occurs between at least one system component and one system environment component where the latter belongs to the system environment of the system to which the system component belongs.
has super-classes
interaction c
has sub-classes
interaction between complex system components and complex system environment components c
is disjoint with
interaction among system components c, interaction among system environment components c

ion exchange chromatography systemc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0000796

A chromatography system used for separating ions in a sample using ion exchange resins in a chromatographic column.
has super-classes
chromatography system c

ion microscopec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0000797

A microscope that uses ions to create high-resolution images of the surface or structure of a sample.
has super-classes
microscope c

irregularly shapedc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PATO_0005020

A shape quality inhering on a bearer by virtue of a bearer's shape lacking in pattern or uniformity; for example, it may have sides of different lengths, different curvatures, or unequal angles.
has super-classes
shape c

isotactic polypropylenec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0050010

polypropylene in which all the methyl groups are aligned on the same side of the polymer chain
has super-classes

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PATO_0000122

A 1-D extent quality which is equal to the distance between two points.
has super-classes
1-D extent c

linearc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PATO_0001199

A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being narrow, with the two opposite margins parallel.
has super-classes
shape c

linear low-density polyethylenec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0050008

polyethylene that is distinguished by its linear backbone with short-chain branching
has super-classes
portion of polyethylene c

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000132

plastic debris particle c

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ENVO_06105109

A role which 1) inheres in a material entity 2) is realised when that entity is discarded outside waste management systems by humans or their technologies.

macro-particle of plasticc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000133

This system of classification refers to that proposed by Hartmann et al. (2019).
A piece of plastic that is 1 cm or more in its largest dimension.
has super-classes
piece of plastic c

macromoleculec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CHEBI_33839

A macromolecule is a molecule of high relative molecular mass, the structure of which essentially comprises the multiple repetition of units derived, actually or conceptually, from molecules of low relative molecular mass.
is equivalent to
polymer molecule c
has super-classes
molecular entity c
has sub-classes
copolymer macromolecule c

macroplastic particlec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ENVO_06105103

A piece of plastic which is between 25 and 1000 millimetres in its longest dimension.
This classification is based on the thresholds detailed in GESAMP report # 99 on recommendation from the SDG 14 indicator team. While the definition is in accordance with the common use of this term (as per GESAMP report # 99), alternative size classifications exist and are detailed in the same report. An alternative classification is a piece of plastic which is between 25 and 50 millimetres in its longest dimension.
Date:
08T14:01:58Z/04/2021
Authors:
https://orcid.org/0000-0003-1072-8081
Contributors:
https://orcid.org/0000-0002-2996-719X
https://orcid.org/0000-0002-8166-0486
has super-classes
piece of plastic c
is disjoint with
microplastic particle - Hartmann et al. (2019) classification c, mesoplastic particle - Hartmann et al. (2019) classification c

macroplastic particle - Hartmann et al. (2019) classificationc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000233

We included an upper-level threshold to completely define this class. We follow EnvO representation.
A plastic debris particle that is between 1 cm and 1 m in its largest dimension.
has super-classes
plastic debris particle - Hartmann et al. (2019) size classification c
is disjoint with

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000183

A manufacturing process of a piece of synthetic plastic that has some portion of recyclable plastic as the main input and some piece of recyclable plastic as the main output.
is equivalent to
manufacturing process of a piece of synthetic plastic c and (has primary output op some piece of recyclable plastic c)
has super-classes
manufacturing process of a piece of synthetic plastic c

manufacturing process of a piece of recycled plasticc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000184

A manufacturing process of a piece of synthetic plastic that has some portion of recycled plastic as the main input and some piece of recycled plastic as the main output.

has super-classes

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000185

manufacturing process of a piece of synthetic plasticc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000226

To date, the ClimOO Ontology does not cover related classes for synthetic polymer-based materials, as out of the current scope. Implementations can go in this direction if needed.
A manufacturing process that has some portion of synthetic plastic as the main input and some piece of synthetic plastic as the main output.
is equivalent to
manufacturing process of a piece of synthetic polymer-based material c and (has primary output op some piece of synthetic plastic c)
has super-classes
manufacturing process of a piece of synthetic polymer-based material c
has sub-classes
manufacturing process of a piece of recyclable plastic c, manufacturing process of a piece of recycled plastic c, manufacturing process of a piece of synthetic biodegradable plastic c, manufacturing process of piece of bio-based plastic c, plastic object manufacturing process c

manufacturing process of a piece of synthetic polymer-based materialc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000277

A material processing that has as a primary input some portion of synthetic polymer-based material and as a primary output a piece of synthetic polymer-based material.
is equivalent to
material processing c and (has primary output op some piece of polymer-based synthetic material c)
has super-classes
material processing c
has sub-classes
manufacturing process of a piece of synthetic plastic c

manufacturing process of piece of bio-based plasticc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000186

A manufacturing process of a piece of synthetic plastic hat has some portion of bio-based plastic material as the main input and some piece of bio-based plastic as the main output.
is equivalent to
manufacturing process of a piece of synthetic plastic c and (has primary output op some piece of bio-based plastic c)
has super-classes
manufacturing process of a piece of synthetic plastic c

massc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PATO_0000125

A physical quality that inheres in a bearer by virtue of the proportion of the bearer's amount of matter.
For any biological use of PATO, the concept that should be used is 'mass' and not 'weight'. For example, increased weight of an organism.
has super-classes
physical quality c

mass percentagec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UO_0000163

A dimensionless concentration unit which denotes the mass of a substance in a mixture as a percentage of the mass of the entire mixture.
has super-classes
mass percentage based unit c

mass percentage based unitc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UO_1000163

has super-classes
unit c
has sub-classes
mass percentage c

mass spectrometerc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0000835

A device used to measure the mass-to-charge ratio of ions to identify and quantify compounds in a sample.
has super-classes
device c

mass volume percentagec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UO_0000164

A dimensionless concentration unit which denotes the mass of the substance in a mixture as a percentage of the volume of the entire mixture.
has super-classes
mass volume percentage based unit c

mass volume percentage based unitc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UO_1000164

has super-classes
unit c
has sub-classes
mass volume percentage c

materialc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0000000

Instances of Portions Of Matter whose shape is relevant for their dispostion to participate in a manufacturing process may be (subclasses of) objects that bear a 'blank role' in the context of the manufacturing process.
A material is a portion of matter that may participate in some manufacturing process and whose shape is not relevant for its participation in the manufacturing process.
has super-classes
portion of matter c
has sub-classes
polymer c

material entityc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000040

An independent continuant that is spatially extended whose identity is independent of that of other entities and can be maintained through time.
(Elucidation) A material entity is an independent continuant has some portion of matter as continuant part
Example 
A human being; the undetached arm of a human being; an aggregate of human beings
has super-classes
independent continuant c
has sub-classes
chemical entity c, fiat object part c, object c, object aggregate c, plastic debris sink c, plastic debris source c, portion of material c, portion of matter c, system c, system component c, system environment c, system environment component c
is in domain of
environs op, has history op, has member part op, has member part at all times op, material basis of op, material basis of at all times op, member part of op, member part of at all times op
is in range of
has material basis op, has material basis at all times op, has member part op, has member part at all times op, history of op, member part of op, member part of at all times op, occurs in op
is disjoint with
immaterial entity c

material processingc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/COB_0000110

A completely executed planned process that intends to output material entities from input material entities
has super-classes
completely executed planned process c
has sub-classes
manufacturing process of a piece of synthetic polymer-based material c, synthetic polymer-based material manufacturing process c

material transformation objectivec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000456

an objective specifiction that creates an specific output object from input materials.
Source
GROUP: OBI PlanAndPlannedProcess Branch
Term status
http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000122
Example 
The objective to create a mouse infected with LCM virus. The objective to create a defined solution of PBS.
has super-classes
objective specification c

maturity levelc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://spec.industrialontologies.org/ontology/core/meta/AnnotationVocabulary/MaturityLevel

classifier used to specify the state of a resource in its development process
has members
provisional ni, released ni

measurement datumc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000109

A measurement datum is an information content entity that is a recording of the output of a measurement such as produced by a device.
Source
OBI_0000305
group:OBI
Term status
http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000125
Example 
Examples of measurement data are the recoding of the weight of a mouse as {40,mass,"grams"}, the recording of an observation of the behavior of the mouse {,process,"agitated"}, the recording of the expression level of a gene as measured through the process of microarray experiment {3.4,luminosity,}.
has super-classes
data item c

Measurement Information Content Entityc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.ontologyrepository.com/CommonCoreOntologies/MeasurementInformationContentEntity

A Descriptive Information Content Entity that describes the extent, dimensions, quantity, or quality of an Entity relative to some standard.
is equivalent to
Descriptive Information Content Entity c and (is a measurement of op some entity c)
has super-classes
Descriptive Information Content Entity c
is in domain of
is a measurement of op
is in range of
is measured by op
is disjoint with
Representational Information Content Entity c

Measurement Unitc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.ontologyrepository.com/CommonCoreOntologies/MeasurementUnit

A Descriptive Information Content Entity that describes a definite magnitude of a physical quantity, defined and adopted by convention and/or by law, that is used as a standard for measurement of the same physical quantity.
has super-classes
Descriptive Information Content Entity c

measurement unit labelc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000003

A measurement unit label is as a label that is part of a scalar measurement datum and denotes a unit of measure.
Term status
http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000125
Example 
Examples of measurement unit labels are liters, inches, weight per volume.
has super-classes
datum label c
has sub-classes
unit c

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000134

This system of classification refers to that proposed by Hartmann et al. (2019).
A piece of plastic that is greater than 1 meter in its largest dimension.
has super-classes
piece of plastic c

megaplastic particlec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ENVO_06105102

A piece of plastic which is greater than 1 metre in its longest dimension.
This classification is based on the thresholds detailed in GESAMP report # 99 on recommendation from the SDG 14 indicator team.

meso-particle of plasticc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000135

This system of classification refers to that proposed by Hartmann et al. (2019).
A piece of plastic that is between 1 and 10 mm in its largest dimension.
has super-classes
piece of plastic c

mesoplastic particlec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ENVO_06105104

A piece of plastic which is between 5 and 25 millimetres in its longest dimension.
This classification is based on the thresholds detailed in GESAMP report # 99 on recommendation from the SDG 14 indicator team. While the definition is in accordance with the common use of this term (as per GESAMP report # 99), alternative size classifications exist. Alternatives classifications are a piece of plastic which is less than 25 millimetres in its longest dimension, or a piece of plastic which is between 1 and 25 millimetres in its longest dimension.
