has super-classes
piece of plastic c
is disjoint with
microplastic particle - Hartmann et al. (2019) classification c

mesoplastic particle - Hartmann et al. (2019) classificationc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000232

A plastic debris particle that is between 1 and 10 mm in its largest dimension.
has super-classes
plastic debris particle - Hartmann et al. (2019) size classification c
is disjoint with
megaplastic particle c, macroplastic particle c, nanoplastic particle c, microplastic particle - Hartmann et al. (2019) classification c, macroplastic particle - Hartmann et al. (2019) classification c, nanoplastic particle - Hartmann et al. (2019) classification c

micro-particle of plasticc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000136

This system of classification refers to that proposed by Hartmann et al. (2019).
A piece of plastic that is between 1 and 1000 µm in its largest dimension.
has super-classes
piece of plastic c

microplastic particlec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ENVO_01000944

A piece of plastic which has a diameter less than five millimetres.
This classification is based on the thresholds detailed in GESAMP report # 99 on recommendation from the SDG 14 indicator team. While the definition is in accordance with the common use of this term (as per GESAMP report # 99), alternative size classifications exist. Alternative classifications are a piece of plastic which is between 1 and 5 millimetres in diameter, a piece of plastic which has a diameter less than 1 millimetre, or a piece of plastic which has a diameter greater than 330 micrometres. The use of the word diameter in this case was understood to mean the generalised form of diameter, i.e. the largest distance between two points belonging to an object.
Contributors:
https://orcid.org/0000-0002-2996-719X
https://orcid.org/0000-0002-8166-0486
https://orcid.org/0000-0003-1072-8081
has super-classes
piece of plastic c
has sub-classes
primary microplastic particle c, secondary microplastic particle c

microplastic particle - Hartmann et al. (2019) classificationc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000230

A possible way to align the two systems of classification concerning microplastic size ranges is to include a “large microplastic” class covering microplastic from 1 to 5 mm. See, for instance, ISO/TR 21960:2020Plastics — Environmental aspects — State of knowledge and methodologies https://www.iso.org/standard/72300.html
A plastic debris particle that is between 1 and 1000 µm in its largest dimension.
has super-classes
plastic debris particle - Hartmann et al. (2019) size classification c
has sub-classes
primary microplastic particle c, secondary microplastic particle c
is disjoint with
megaplastic particle c, macroplastic particle c, mesoplastic particle c, nanoplastic particle c, mesoplastic particle - Hartmann et al. (2019) classification c, macroplastic particle - Hartmann et al. (2019) classification c, nanoplastic particle - Hartmann et al. (2019) classification c

microplastic pollution processc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000137

A plastic pollution process during which microplastic debris is released into an environment.
has super-classes
plastic pollution process c

microplastic sorption process of pollutantsc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000138

A synthetic polymer-based material sorption of pollutants that has as output some portion of synthetic polymer-based material that composes some microplastic debris particle with an increased level of pollutants as part (compared to levels prior to participating in that process).
is equivalent to
plastic debris sorption process of pollutants c and (has output op some (portion of synthetic polymer-based material c and (continuant part of op some microplastic particle - Hartmann et al. (2019) classification c)) and (has increased levels of op some chemical entity c and (has role op some environmental contaminant c)))
has super-classes
plastic debris sorption process of pollutants c

microscopec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0000855

A device used to magnify and view small objects or details that are not visible to the naked eye.
has super-classes
device c
has sub-classes
atomic force microscope c, electron microscope c, ion microscope c, optical microscope c

mineralization processc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000139

Definitions of plastic properties, such as biodegradability and composability, and related processes, currently covered in the ClimOO Ontology, refer to existing policy standards. This aims to stress the significance of such regulations concerning defining such entities and, especially, the consequences in terms of pollution and sustainability interventions. It is noteworthy to mention that such standards present limitations. See, for instance, the work of Rayns et al. (2021), Steiner et al. (2022, 2024), concerning plastic biodegradability. We envision implementing such definitions in accordance with regulation revisions and novel research findings.
A process that leads some organic substance to be impregnated by or turned into inorganic substance.
Source
Vert, M., Doi, Y., Hellwich, K.-H., Hess, M., Hodge, P., Kubisa, P., Rinaudo, M., & Schué, F. (2012). Terminology for biorelated polymers and applications (IUPAC Recommendations 2012). Pure Appl. Chem., 84(2), 377–410.
has super-classes
process c
has sub-classes
polymer biomineralization process c

mixturec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CHEBI_60004

A mixture is a chemical substance composed of multiple molecules, at least two of which are of a different kind.
has super-classes
chemical substance c
has sub-classes
polymer c

modified natural polymer moleculec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000140

References to slightly natural polymers can be added as needed.
A synthetic polymer molecule that derives from a natural polymer molecule processed and altered through human intervention.
has super-classes
synthetic polymer molecule c

mole fractionc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UO_0000076

A concentration unit which denotes the number of moles of solute as a proportion of the total number of moles in a solution.
has super-classes
mole fraction based unit c

mole fraction based unitc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UO_1000076

has super-classes
unit c
has sub-classes
mole fraction c

molecular entityc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CHEBI_23367

Any constitutionally or isotopically distinct atom, molecule, ion, ion pair, radical, radical ion, complex, conformer etc., identifiable as a separately distinguishable entity.
has super-classes
chemical entity c
has sub-classes
macromolecule c, monomer c, polymer molecule c, residue molecule of synthetic plastic manufacturing c, synthetic polymer-based material additive c

monomerc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000141

A molecular entity that bears a disposition to polymerize.
has super-classes
molecular entity c

morphologyc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PATO_0000051

A quality of a single physical entity inhering in the bearer by virtue of the bearer's size or shape or structure.
has super-classes
physical object quality c
has sub-classes
amorphous c, shape c, size c, structure c

nano-particle of plasticc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000142

This system of classification refers to that proposed by Hartmann et al. (2019).
A piece of plastic that is between 1 and 1000 nm in its largest dimension.
has super-classes
piece of plastic c

nanoplastic particlec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ENVO_06105105

A piece of plastic which has a diameter less than 1 micrometre.
This classification is based on the thresholds detailed in GESAMP report # 99 on recommendation from the SDG 14 indicator team. While the definition is in accordance with the common use of this term (as per GESAMP report # 99), alternative size classifications exist. An alternative classification is a piece of plastic which has a diameter less than 100 nanometres. The GESAMP report states that it assumes a spherical shape for this size classification, which was understood to mean the generalised form of diameter, i.e. the largest distance between two points belonging to an object.

natural rubberc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0050031

elastomer polymer, and a biopolymer harvested as plant latex; consisting of cis-1,4-polyisoprene chains; typically sulfur-vulcanized to create crosslinks between chains
has super-classes
elastomer c

nitrile butadiene rubberc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0050034

synthetic rubber that belongs to a family of synthetic acrylonitrile–butadiene copolymer elastomers
Example 
 it is widely used in oil/fuel resistance applicaitons such as seals, fuel hoses, and protective gloves
has super-classes
synthetic rubber c

novolac epoxyc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0050025

epoxy resin that is based on novolac resins to provide improved thermal and chemical resistance
has super-classes
epoxy resin c

nurdlec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ENVO_01000777

A plastic pellet which is produced such that its size allows it to be rapidly melted and used as input for further downstream manufacturing processes.
Contributors:
https://orcid.org/0000-0002-2996-719X
https://orcid.org/0000-0002-4366-3088
has super-classes
plastic pellet c

objectc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000030

(Elucidation) An object is a material entity which manifests causal unity & is of a type instances of which are maximal relative to the sort of causal unity manifested
Example 
An organism; a fish tank; a planet; a laptop; a valve; a block of marble; an ice cube
has super-classes
material entity c
has sub-classes
device c, piece of plastic c, piece of polymer-based material c

object aggregatec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000027

(Elucidation) An object aggregate is a material entity consisting exactly of a plurality (≥1) of objects as member parts which together form a unit
Example 
The aggregate of the musicians in a symphony orchestra and their instruments; the aggregate of bearings in a constant velocity axle joint; the nitrogen atoms in the atmosphere; a collection of cells in a blood biobank
has super-classes
material entity c
has sub-classes
connected material entity aggregate c, disconnected material entity aggregate c, plastic debris aggregate c

objective specificationc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000005

A directive information entity that describes an intended process endpoint. When part of a plan specification the concretization is realized in a planned process in which the bearer tries to effect the world so that the process endpoint is achieved.
Source
OBI Plan and Planned Process/Roles Branch
OBI_0000217
Term status
http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000122
Example 
In the protocol of a ChIP assay the objective specification says to identify protein and DNA interaction.
has super-classes
directive information entity c
has sub-classes
material transformation objective c
is in domain of
objective_achieved_by op
is in range of
achieves_planned_objective op

occurrentc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000003

An entity that has temporal parts and that happens, unfolds or develops through time.
(Elucidation) An occurrent is an entity that unfolds itself in time or it is the start or end of such an entity or it is a temporal or spatiotemporal region
Example 
As for process, history, process boundary, spatiotemporal region, zero-dimensional temporal region, one-dimensional temporal region, temporal interval, temporal instant.
has super-classes
entity c
has sub-classes
process c, process boundary c, spatiotemporal region c, temporal region c
is in domain of
has occurrent part op, has proper occurrent part op, has proper temporal part op, has temporal part op, occurrent part of op, preceded by op, precedes op, proper occurrent part of op, proper temporal part of op, temporal part of op
is in range of
has occurrent part op, has proper occurrent part op, has proper temporal part op, has temporal part op, occurrent part of op, preceded by op, precedes op, proper occurrent part of op, proper temporal part of op, temporal part of op
is disjoint with
continuant c

one-dimensional spatial regionc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000026

(Elucidation) A one-dimensional spatial region is a whole consisting of a line together with zero or more lines which may have points as parts
Example 
An edge of a cube-shaped portion of space; a line connecting two points; two parallel lines extended in space
has super-classes
spatial region c
is disjoint with
two-dimensional spatial region c, zero-dimensional spatial region c, three-dimensional spatial region c

one-dimensional temporal regionc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000038

(Elucidation) A one-dimensional temporal region is a temporal region that is a whole that has a temporal interval and zero or more temporal intervals and temporal instants as parts
Example 
The temporal region during which a process occurs
has super-classes
temporal region c
has sub-classes
temporal interval c
is disjoint with
zero-dimensional temporal region c

optical microscopec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0000874

A microscope that uses visible light and lenses to magnify and view small objects or details.
has super-classes
microscope c

Optical Propertyc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.ontologyrepository.com/CommonCoreOntologies/OpticalProperty

An Electromagnetic Radiation Property that is realized when its bearer interacts with electromagnetic waves within the visible light spectrum.
has super-classes
Electromagnetic Radiation Property c
has sub-classes
Color c

Orangec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.ontologyrepository.com/CommonCoreOntologies/Orange

A Color that is between Red and Yellow with a wavelength in the visible spectrum typically between 590 to 635 nanometers.
has super-classes
Color c

output specificationc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0000015

A parameter specification that prescribes a data item intended to be produced as output by the execution of a workflow function.
has super-classes
parameter specification c

parameter specificationc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0000013

A directive information entity that specifies a parameter required or produced by a workflow function, including its intended role, position, and constraints.
has super-classes
has sub-classes
input specification c, output specification c

persistent organic pollutantc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CHEBI_77853

Any environmental contaminant that is resistant to environmental degradation through photolytic, biological or chemical processes. Such substances can have significant impact on health and the environment, as they persist in the environment, bioaccumulate in animal tissue and so biomagnify in food chains.
has super-classes
environmental contaminant c

perspectivec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/sco#SCO_0000016

SCO currently accounts for perspectives of complex system components and complex system environment components because the focus of this ontology is on complex systems. Future versions of this ontology may explore extending reference more broadly to systems.
A disposition whose realization leads the complex system component or the complex system environment component to (re)position itself within and regarding the complex system or complex system environment it belongs to (or a part of it) and possibly act accordingly.
has super-classes
disposition c
has sub-classes
stakeholder perspective c
has members
ClimOO project perspective on plastics ni, EnvO perspective on microplastics ni, European Union and Italy perspective on plastics, plastic pollution, biodegradability, and compostability ni, Hartmann et al. (2019) perspective on microplastics ni, Perspective of GESAMP 2019 report on microplastics ni, Rochman et al. (2019) perspective on microplastics ni

perspective information content entityc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/sco#SCO_0000004

An information content entity that generically depends on some complex system component or complex system environment component and is about the perspective (that concretizes it) of that complex system component or complex system environment component.
is equivalent to
Information Content Entity c and (is about op some perspective c)
has super-classes
Information Content Entity c
has sub-classes
stakeholder perspective information content entity c
is disjoint with
Representational Information Content Entity c, worth information content entity c

IRI: https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0050027

phenolic resin that is synthesized from phenol and formaldehyde
has super-classes
phenolic resin c

IRI: https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0050026

thermosetting polymer synthesized via condensation of phenol and formaldehyde
has super-classes

has sub-classes
phenol-formaldehyde resin c

A quality which inheres in a continuant.
has super-classes
quality c, quality c
has sub-classes
morphology c, physical quality c

physical qualityc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PATO_0001018

A quality of a physical entity that exists through action of continuants at the physical level of organisation in relation to other entities.

mass c, position c, quality of a substance c, temperature c

piece of inorganic or hybrid polymer-based synthetic materialc back to ToC or Class ToC

piece of polymer-based synthetic material c

piece of plasticc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ENVO_01000776

A mass of solid material which is primarily composed of plastic.
ClimOO Ontology: We imported this hierarchy of EnvO classes with the purpose of including an alternative system of classification of plastic debris particles and mapping it with that proposed by Hartmann et al. (2019), covered within the ClimOO Ontology. The alignment applies only with regard to particle size as a matter of example. EnvO classes for plastic debris particles do not present axioms besides the subsumption relation to the “piece of plastic” class. Class meaning concerning particle size only resides within the definitions. We import the “piece of plastic” class without including the related axiomatization and subsume this class directly to BFO: object class, as we imported it solely for grouping purposes. It is not relevant for the alignment. We envision discussions with EnvO's curators to consolidate this alignment, especially in terms of class axiomatization.
Contributors:
https://orcid.org/0000-0002-2996-719X
https://orcid.org/0000-0002-8166-0486
https://orcid.org/0000-0003-1072-8081
has super-classes
object c
has sub-classes
macroplastic particle c, megaplastic particle c, mesoplastic particle c, microplastic particle c, nanoplastic particle c, piece of plastic foam c, plastic film c, plastic fragment c, plastic line c, plastic pellet c

piece of plasticc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000253

Not all plastics are solid and not under all conditions and temperatures.
A piece of polymer-based material that is made of plastic.
has super-classes
piece of polymer-based material c
has sub-classes
macro-particle of plastic c, mega-particle of plastic c, meso-particle of plastic c, micro-particle of plastic c, nano-particle of plastic c, piece of biodegradable plastic c, plastic fragment c, plastic line c

piece of plastic foamc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ENVO_06105006

A piece of plastic which has a matrix- or sponge-like structure with numerous, hollow internal spaces.
Depending on the type of plastic used and the organisation of the foam, a piece of plastic foam can show elastic properties (even if the plastic it is made out of does not have elastic properties).

planc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000260

A plan is a realizable entity that is the inheres in a bearer who is committed to realizing it as a completely executed planned process.
Source
branch derived
Term status
http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000125
Example 
The plan of researcher X to perform an experiment according to a protocol.
has super-classes
realizable entity c

plan specificationc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000104

A directive information entity with action specifications and objective specifications as parts, and that may be concretized as a realizable entity that, if realized, is realized in a process in which the bearer tries to achieve the objectives by taking the actions specified.
2/3/2009 Comment from OBI review. Action specification not well enough specified. Conditional specification not well enough specified. Question whether all plan specifications have objective specifications. Request that IAO either clarify these or change definitions not to use them
Source
OBI Plan and Planned Process branch
OBI_0000344
Term status
http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000122
Example 
PMID: 18323827.Nat Med. 2008 Mar;14(3):226.New plan proposed to help resolve conflicting medical advice.
has super-classes
directive information entity c

planned processc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/COB_0000082

A process that is initiated by an agent who intends to carry out a plan to achieve an objective through one or more actions as described in a plan specification.
has super-classes
process c
has sub-classes
completely executed planned process c, failed planned process c

planned transportation process of a piece of synthetic plasticc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000285

A planned transportation process that concerns a piece of synthetic plastic.
has super-classes
planned transportation process of a piece of synthetic polymer-based material c
has sub-classes
transportation process of a plastic debris particle c, transportation process of a plastic object c

planned transportation process of a piece of synthetic polymer-based materialc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000284

A transportation process that concerns a piece of synthetic polymer-based material.
has super-classes
transportation process c
has sub-classes
planned transportation process of a piece of synthetic plastic c

planned transportation process of a portion of synthetic plasticc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000283

A planned transportation process of a portion of synthetic polymer-based material that concerns a portion of synthetic plastic.
has super-classes

planned transportation process of a portion of synthetic polymer-based materialc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000282

A transportation process that concerns a portion of synthetic polymer-based material.
has super-classes
transportation process c

plastic additivec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000156

A molecule with a synthetic polymer-based material additive role that is added to some synthetic plastic polymers.
is equivalent to
synthetic polymer-based material additive c and (continuant part of op some portion of synthetic plastic material c)
has super-classes
synthetic polymer-based material additive c

plastic biomineralization processc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000157

We primarily focused on mineralization processes in relation to synthetic plastics, related polymers and items. Reference to such processes concerning plastics more in general, for instance in relation to natural plastics, or in relation to polymer-based materials can be added as needed.
A polymer biomineralization process that applies to some biodegradable polymer molecules constituting some portion of biodegradable plastic
is equivalent to
polymer biomineralization process c and (has input op some biodegradable polymer molecule c and (continuant part of at all times op some portion of biodegradable plastic material c))
has super-classes
polymer biomineralization process c
has sub-classes
synthetic plastic biomineralization process c

plastic combustion processc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000158

We currently focus this class on pieces of synthetic plastic. Implementations will expand current representations to plastic materials and polymers. Also, reference to the combustion of pieces of synthetic polymer-based materials can be included, as needed.
An exothermic chemical reaction process between some plastic and an oxidant (usually oxygen), which is sometimes accompanied by the production of light.
has super-classes
process c

plastic composting processc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000159

A composting process that applies to polymer molecules constituting portions of plastic of which some waste piece of biodegradable synthetic plastic, used as input or part of the input of this process, is made. Packaging applications: The pieces of biodegradable synthetic plastic must be broken down into particles smaller than 2 mm in their largest dimension within the first 12 weeks (according to EU EN 13432, standardized laboratory test conditions). Presence of heavy metals in the output compost must be below harmful levels with no negative consequences concerning agronomic value and plant growth ecotoxicity (See EU standard directly). (See also UNI EN ISO 20200 and Regulation - 2023/2055 - EN - EUR-Lex).
is equivalent to
composting process c and (has input op some (biodegradable polymer molecule c and synthetic polymer molecule c) and (continuant part of at all times op some portion of compostable synthetic plastic material c and (continuant part of at all times op some piece of compostable synthetic plastic c and (has role op some waste role c))))
has super-classes
biodegradation process of synthetic plastic c

plastic debris aggregatec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000160

References to plastic debris aggregate formation and participation in pollution processes can be added as needed.
An aggregate that has member part at least a plastic debris.
is equivalent to
object aggregate c and (has member part at all times op some plastic debris particle c)
has super-classes
object aggregate c

plastic debris degradation processc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000161

An polymer degradation process that leads some polymer molecules constituting some portion of polymer-based material composing some plastic debris to vary their physical and chemical qualities due to interactions with factors such as heat, light, or chemical entities, resulting in a decrease in their molecular weight.
is equivalent to
degradation process of a piece of synthetic polymer-based material c and (has input op some synthetic polymer molecule c and (continuant part of at all times op some portion of polymer-based material c and (continuant part of at all times op some plastic debris particle c)))
has super-classes
degradation process of a piece of synthetic polymer-based material c

plastic debris fragmentation processc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000162

A fragmentation process of a piece of synthetic polymer-based material process that leads to breaking pieces of plastic debris down into pieces of a smaller size.
has super-classes
fragmentation process of a piece of synthetic polymer-based material c

plastic debris particlec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000163

Classification systems for plastic debris vary, especially according to particle size. For instance, EnvO considers 5 mm as the upper-level threshold to define microplastic particles, as this is a commonly recognized size edge in discussions on microplastic litter. Nevertheless, this is merely an operative definition, and there is no complete consensus on employing it (Hartmann et al., 2019). Hartmann et al. (2019) propose a range of 1-1000 micrometers. For illustrative purposes, the ClimOO ontology includes EnvO classification of plastic debris particles and maps it with that proposed by Hartmann et al. (2019), (covered by the ClimOO Ontology). The focus of the mpping is only on prticle size. See also comments to EnvO "piece of plastic" class. These classifications differentiate for the size range of micro-, macro-, and meso-plastic particles. The shared size ranges for mega- and nano-plastic particles. This is also intended to be an initial guideline to align and map different classification systems.
a piece of synthetic polymer-based material that, at a certain time, is 1) solid, 2) not soluble in water (water solubility), 3) persistent in an environment (bearing a disposition to environmental persistence), generally as pollutant, 4) mobile (bearing a disposition to motion in the environment), and 5) is no longer used for a purpose.
is equivalent to
piece of polymer-based synthetic material c and (has quality op some quality of a solid c) and (has role op some environmental contaminant c) and (has disposition op some environmental persistence c) and (has disposition op some disposition to motion in the environment c) and (has disposition op some not (water solubility c))
has super-classes
piece of polymer-based synthetic material c
has sub-classes
film debris particle c, linear plastic debris particle c, plastic debris particle - Hartmann et al. (2019) size classification c, plastic pellet debris particle c, plastic surface coating debris particle c, tire wear particle c

plastic debris particle - Hartmann et al. (2019) size classificationc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000231

This class has only been created for grouping purposes. Do not use it directly.
has super-classes
plastic debris particle c
has sub-classes
macroplastic particle - Hartmann et al. (2019) classification c, mesoplastic particle - Hartmann et al. (2019) classification c, microplastic particle - Hartmann et al. (2019) classification c, nanoplastic particle - Hartmann et al. (2019) classification c

plastic debris sinkc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000164

plastic debris sink may be temporary or permanent.
A material entity with a plastic debris sink role.
is equivalent to
material entity c and (has role op some plastic debris sink role c)
has super-classes
material entity c

plastic debris sink rolec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000165

A role that inheres in a material entity (normally not a portion of plastics or polymer-based synthetic material) that participates in a plastic pollution process.
has super-classes
role c

plastic debris sorption process of pollutantsc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000237

A synthetic polymer-based material sorption of pollutants that has as output some portion of synthetic polymer-based material that composes some plastic debris particle with an increased level of pollutants as part (compared to levels prior to participating in that process).
is equivalent to
synthetic polymer-based material sorption process of pollutants c and (has output op some portion of synthetic polymer-based material c and ((continuant part of op some plastic debris particle c) and (has increased levels of op some chemical entity c and (has role op some environmental contaminant c))))
has super-classes
synthetic polymer-based material sorption process of pollutants c
has sub-classes
microplastic sorption process of pollutants c

plastic debris sourcec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000166

From a material science perspective (which is the one we have to refer to for classifying plastics and polymers) plastic materials are constituted by polymers that are thermosetting and thermoplastic, and not, for instance, by elastic polymers. There exist, however, some synthetic combinations, such as thermoplastic elastomer-based synthetic materials, that are composed by polymers presenting both elastic and plastic properties. Strictly speaking, the last are not plastics (material science perspective). However, these materials are included in the count of debris (from an environmental perspective which is the one we have to refer to for classifying debris and pollution). So, the ClimOO Ontology accounts for these two perspectives, modeling differences and interconnection.
A material entity that has as part some polymer-based synthetic material and bears some plastic debris source role. It is generally a piece of synthetic plastic, especially plastic objects.
is equivalent to
material entity c and (has continuant part at all times op some piece of polymer-based synthetic material c) and (has role op some plastic debris source role c)
has super-classes
material entity c

plastic debris source rolec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000167

A role that inheres in a material entity having as part some portion of polymer-based synthetic material that participates in a plastic pollution process.
has super-classes
role c

plastic debris weatheringc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000296

A weathering process of a piece of synthetic polymer-based material that applies to a plastic debris particle.
has super-classes
weathering process of a piece of synthetic polymer-based material c

plastic degradation processc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000168

An process that leads some polymer molecules constituting some portion of plastic to vary their physical and chemical qualities due to interactions with factors such as heat, light, or chemical entities, resulting in a decrease in their molecular weight.
is equivalent to
polymer degradation process c and (has input op some polymer molecule c and (continuant part of at all times op some portion of plastic material c))
has super-classes
polymer degradation process c
has sub-classes
biodegradation process of plastic c

plastic filmc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ENVO_06105007

A piece of plastic which is flexible, thin, and planar.
The edge can be either smooth or angular.

has super-classes
piece of plastic c

plastic filmc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000255

A piece of synthetic plastic which is flexible, thin, and planar.
has super-classes
piece of synthetic plastic c

plastic fragmentc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ENVO_06105008

A piece of plastic which 1) is hard, 2) has an irregular shape, and 3) has broken off of a larger piece of plastic.
To avoid ambiguity, we are being stricter with the "has broken off of a larger piece of plastic" aspect compared to the GESAMP report, which originally stated "having appearance of being broken down from a larger piece of litter".
Date:
04T14:37:37Z/05/2021
Authors:
https://orcid.org/0000-0002-8166-0486
Contributors:
https://orcid.org/0000-0002-2996-719X
https://orcid.org/0000-0003-1072-8081
has super-classes
piece of plastic c

plastic fragmentc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000256

A piece of plastic that is an output of a plastic fragmentation process.
is equivalent to
piece of plastic c and (output of op some plastic fragmentation process c)
has super-classes
piece of plastic c

plastic fragmentation processc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000169

Definitions of plastic properties, such as biodegradability and composability, and related processes, currently covered in the ClimOO Ontology, refer to existing policy standards. This aims to stress the significance of such regulations concerning defining such entities and, especially, the consequences in terms of pollution and sustainability interventions. It is noteworthy to mention that such standards present limitations. See, for instance, the work of Rayns et al. (2021), Steiner et al. (2022, 2024), concerning plastic biodegradability. We envision implementing such definitions in accordance with regulation revisions and novel research findings.
A process that leads to breaking pieces of plastic down into pieces of a smaller size.
has super-classes
fragmentation process of a piece of polymer-based material c
has sub-classes
synthetic plastic fragmentation process c

plastic linec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ENVO_06105009

A piece of plastic which is linear and flexible.

04T14:40:36Z/05/2021
Authors:
https://orcid.org/0000-0002-8166-0486
Contributors:
https://orcid.org/0000-0002-2996-719X
https://orcid.org/0000-0003-1072-8081
has super-classes
piece of plastic c

plastic linec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000257

A piece of plastic which is linear and flexible.
has super-classes
piece of plastic c

plastic manufacturing processc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000278

A synthetic polymer-based material manufacturing process that has as a primary output a portion of synthetic plastic.
is equivalent to
synthetic polymer-based material manufacturing process c and (has primary output op some portion of synthetic plastic material c)
has super-classes
synthetic polymer-based material manufacturing process c
has sub-classes
bio-based plastic manufacturing process c, biodegradable synthetic plastic manufacturing process c, recyclable plastic manufacturing process c, recycled plastic manufacturing process c

plastic nurdlec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000259

A plastic pellet that is manufactured in such a way that its size enables quick melting thereby making it usable for further downstream manufacturing processes.
has super-classes
plastic pellet c

plastic objectc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000171

Further details concerning stakeholders and objective and action specifications can be added to the axiomatixation of this class, as needed.
A piece of synthetic plastic that bears some function(s).
is equivalent to
piece of synthetic plastic c and (bearer of op some function c)
has super-classes
piece of synthetic plastic c

plastic object manufacturing processc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000172

References to manufacturing processes of plastic objects made of specific type of plastics, such as recycled, virgin, biobased, and biodegradable can be added as needed.
A manufacturing process of a piece of synthetic plastic that has a plastic object as the main output.
is equivalent to
manufacturing process of a piece of synthetic plastic c and (has primary output op some plastic object c)
has super-classes
manufacturing process of a piece of synthetic plastic c

plastic object usage processc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000173

A usage process of some piece of synthetic polymer-based material where at least one of the involved pieces of synthetic polymer-based material is a plastic object employed for a purpose.
has super-classes
usage process of some piece of synthetic plastic c

plastic pelletc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ENVO_06105010

A piece of plastic which is 1) hard, 2) has a smooth external surface, and 3) has a roughly spherical or granular shape.

plastic pelletc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000258

Pellets are normally produced as such. They are often used as raw materials for further manufacturing processes. Pellets are of a small size (threshold to be defined).
A piece of solid (at room temperature) synthetic plastic which is 1) hard, 2) has a smooth external surface, and 3) has a roughly spherical or granular shape.
has super-classes
piece of synthetic plastic c
has sub-classes
plastic nurdle c

plastic pellet debris particlec back to ToC or Class ToC

A plastic pellet that has been released into an environmental material.
has super-classes
plastic debris particle c

plastic pollution prevention processc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000238

Extensions of the model will address 3R and 6R principles frameworks.
A planned process that avoids the release of plastic debris particles into a plastic debris sink, suppressing some plastic pollution process.
has super-classes
completely executed planned process c

plastic pollution processc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000266

Implementations will expand current representation to distinguish intentional from unintentional plastic pollution processes.
A process during which plastic debris particles are released into and possibly accumulated in an environment, from some plastic debris source to some plastic debris sink.
has super-classes
process c
has sub-classes
microplastic pollution process c

plastic recycling processc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000175

A recycling process of synthetic polymer molecules that applies to some recyclable polymer molecules constituting a portion of synthetic plastic material composing some plastic waste.
is equivalent to
recycling process of synthetic polymer molecules c and (has primary input op some recyclable polymer molecule c and (continuant part of at all times op some portion of synthetic plastic material c and (continuant part of at all times op some piece of synthetic plastic c and (has role op some waste role c))))
has super-classes

plastic sorption process of pollutantsc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000176

The focus is on synthetic plastics here.
A synthetic polymer-based material sorption of pollutants that has as output some synthetic plastic with an increased level of pollutants as part (compared to levels prior to participating in that process).
is equivalent to
synthetic polymer-based material sorption process of pollutants c and (has output op some portion of synthetic plastic material c and (has increased levels of op some chemical entity c and (has role op some environmental contaminant c)))
has super-classes
synthetic polymer-based material sorption process of pollutants c

plastic surface coating debris particlec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000177

A debris particle of polymer composite-based synthetic material that is or derives from some plastic-based surface coatings, such as paints, and has been released into some environmental material.
has super-classes
plastic debris particle c

plastic upcycling processc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000178

Currently, the ClimOO Ontology imports EnvO representation of waste role and includes a class for plastic upcycling. This is adequate for current ontology purposes. Given the strict interconnection between upcycling and recycling processes, however, we envision discussions with subject matter expert for possible implementations.
A material processing that has as an input some piece of synthetic plastic or corresponding waste, as output at least one material entity to which some stakehoder ascribes a higher value (compared to the value of the plastic input).
has super-classes

plastic weatheringc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000294

A weathering process of a piece of synthetic polymer-based material that applies to a piece of plastic.
has super-classes
weathering process of a piece of synthetic polymer-based material c

poisonc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CHEBI_64909

Any substance that causes disturbance to organisms by chemical reaction or other activity on the molecular scale, when a sufficient quantity is absorbed by the organism.
has super-classes
biological role c

polybutylene succinatec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0050039

biodegradable thermoplastic polymer polyester that is synthesized via the polycondensation of succinic acid and 1,4-butanediol
has super-classes
portion of biodegradable plastic material c, thermoplastic polymer c

polychloroprenc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0090053

synthetic rubber that belongs to the family of synthetic elastomeric rubbers; synthesised typically through emulsion polymerization of chloroprene
Example 
 it is widely used in gaskets and wetsuits.
has super-classes
synthetic rubber c

polyethylene terephthalatec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0050019

thermoplastic polymer produced by polycondensation of ethylene glycol and terephthalate precursors
has super-classes
thermoplastic polymer c
has sub-classes
amorphous polyethylene terephthalate c, crystalline polyethylene terephthalate c

polyhydroxyalkanoatesc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0050038

polyhydroxyalkanoates are biodegradable polymers that are biosynthesized by microorganisms from sugars or lipids
has super-classes
polymer c

polylactic acidc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0050037

ClimOO Ontology comment: We did not include the PMDco class for biodegradable polymers because PMDco erroneously refers to biodegradability in the portions of material and not to the constituting polymer molecules. We, however, imported PMDco “biodegradability” class and mapped it as equivalent to ClimOO “biodegradability” class. We also include the PMDco “polybutylene succinate” and “polylactic acid” classes, subclasses of and mapped as subclasses of the ClimOO “portion of biodegradable plastic” class. This aims to be an initial mapping till PMDco revises its class. See: https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0050036 https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0200001
biodegradable thermoplastic polymer produced from renewable resources such as corn starch
has super-classes
portion of biodegradable plastic material c, thermoplastic polymer c

polymerc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CHEBI_60027

A polymer is a mixture, which is composed of macromolecules of different kinds and which may be differentiated by composition, length, degree of branching etc..
ClimOO Ontology comment: ChEBI covers several subclasses that may be relevant for inclusion, such as polyester or rubber polymer materials. We refer users to ChEBI for importing as needed.
is equivalent to
portion of polymer-based material c
has super-classes
mixture c
has sub-classes
copolymer c, synthetic polymer c

polymerc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0000888

ClimOO Ontology comment: Till release 3.0.0-rc2, despite acknowledging the existence of natural polymer materials, PMDco focused the polymer classes on those of synthetic production. In the current release (3.0.0), PMDco changed the subsumption axiomatization of the “polymer” class from being a subclass of the “engineered material” class to the “material” class. We assume to extend its coverage to also include non-synthetic polymer materials (see included subclasses)and map accordingly. The definition of the PMDco “polymer” class, however, has not been changed yet, still referring to engineered materials.
A polymer is an engineered material that consists of many repeated subunits (monomers) to form long chains. The atoms within a polymer chain are bonded together by covalent bonds. The intermolecular forces between polymer chains are typically represented by van der waals forces and hydrogen bonds.
is equivalent to
portion of polymer-based material c
material c and (output of op some polymerization c)
has super-classes
material c
has sub-classes
elastomer c, polyhydroxyalkanoates c, thermoplastic polymer c, thermosetting polymer c

polymer biodegradation processc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000180

Definitions of plastic properties, such as biodegradability and composability, and related processes, currently covered in the ClimOO Ontology, refer to existing policy standards. This aims to stress the significance of such regulations concerning defining such entities and, especially, the consequences in terms of pollution and sustainability interventions. It is noteworthy to mention that such standards present limitations. See, for instance, the work of Rayns et al. (2021), Steiner et al. (2022, 2024), concerning plastic biodegradability. We envision implementing such definitions in accordance with regulation revisions and novel research findings.
A biodegradation process that applies to some polymer molecules (biodegradable polymer molecules).
is equivalent to
polymer degradation process c and (has input op some biodegradable polymer molecule c)
has super-classes
biodegradation process c

polymer biomineralization processc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000271

A mineralization process that applies to some biodegradable polymer molecules and is mediated by living organisms (no humans).
has super-classes
mineralization process c
has sub-classes
plastic biomineralization process c

polymer degradation processc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000269

Definitions of plastic properties, such as biodegradability and composability, and related processes, currently covered in the ClimOO Ontology, refer to existing policy standards. This aims to stress the significance of such regulations concerning defining such entities and, especially, the consequences in terms of pollution and sustainability interventions. It is noteworthy to mention that such standards present limitations. See, for instance, the work of Rayns et al. (2021), Steiner et al. (2022, 2024), concerning plastic biodegradability. We envision implementing such definitions in accordance with regulation revisions and novel research findings.
A process that leads some polymer molecules to vary their physical and chemical qualities due to interactions with factors such as heat, light, or chemical entities, resulting in a decrease in their molecular weight.
has super-classes
process c
has sub-classes
plastic degradation process c, synthetic polymer-based material degradation process c

polymer moleculec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000242

Currently, there is no standard-length threshold in the literature.
A polymer molecule is a long chain of macromolecules, usually of different species, named monomers, that is synthesized through polymerization processes (it is generally a result of a polymerization process; it may be the case that a polymer molecule derives from the breaking of a longer molecule.
has super-classes
molecular entity c
has sub-classes
biodegradable polymer molecule c, elastomer molecule c, inorganic or hybrid polymer molecule c, synthetic polymer molecule c, thermoplastic polymer molecule c, thermosetting polymer molecule c

polymerizationc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0010033

The chemical process of reacting monomers, oligomers, or reactive precursors together to form longer macromolecular chains or three-dimensional networks
has super-classes
process c
has sub-classes
polymerization process c

polymerization processc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000182

A process that converts some input monomer molecule (at least two) into some polymer molecules.
Source
Compendium of polymer terminology and nomenclature: IUPAC recommendations, 2008. (2009). Choice (Middletown), 47(01), 47-0284-47–0284. Vert, M., Doi, Y., Hellwich, K.-H., Hess, M., Hodge, P., Kubisa, P., Rinaudo, M., & Schué, F. (2012). Terminology for biorelated polymers and applications (IUPAC Recommendations 2012). Pure Appl. Chem., 84(2), 377–410.
has super-classes
polymerization c
has sub-classes
synthetic polymerization process c

polypropylenec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0050009

thermoplastic polymer produced by polymerization of the monomer propylene including further crosslinking and modifications using other comonomers
has super-classes
thermoplastic polymer c
has sub-classes
atactic polypropylene c, isotactic polypropylene c, syndiotactic polypropylene c

polystyrenec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0050016

thermoplastic polymer produced by polymerization of the aromatic hydrocarbon styrene
has super-classes
thermoplastic polymer c
has sub-classes
general purpose polystyrene c, high impact polystyrene c

polyvinyl chloridec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0050013

thermoplastic polymer produced by polymerization of the monomer vinyl chloride
has super-classes
thermoplastic polymer c
has sub-classes
flexible polyvinyl chloride c, rigid polyvinyl chloride c

portion of amorphous solid materialc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000245

A portion of solid material that does not have a regularly organised internal structure.
is equivalent to
portion of solid material c and (has quality op some amorphous c or unstructured c)
has super-classes
portion of solid material c
has sub-classes
portion of compost c

portion of bio-based plastic materialc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000097

A portion of synthetic plastic material that is composed of biobased polymer molecules.
has super-classes
portion of synthetic plastic material c

portion of biodegradable plastic materialc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000107

A portion of plastic material that is constituted by biodegradable polymer molecules, is the main input of a process of biodegradation of plastic.
has super-classes
portion of plastic material c
has sub-classes
polybutylene succinate c, polylactic acid c, portion of synthetic biodegradable plastic material c

portion of compostc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000114

A portion of amorphous solid material that is obtained by composting organic wastes, generally composed of vegetable, fruit, and garden residues, sometimes with other organic materials. The input wastes contain a limited amount of minerals. (See EU EN 13432, UNI EN ISO 20200, and Regulation - 2023/2055 - EN - EUR-Lex).
is equivalent to
portion of amorphous solid material c and (output of op some composting process c)
has super-classes
portion of amorphous solid material c

portion of compostable synthetic plastic materialc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000117

A portion of biodegradable synthetic plastic material that is copmosed of polymer molecules that bear a disposition to be degraded during a plastic composting process (compostability).
has super-classes
portion of synthetic biodegradable plastic material c

portion of copolymer-based synthetic materialc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000121

A portion of synthetic polymer-based material that is composed of some synthetic copolymer molecules.
has super-classes
portion of synthetic polymer-based material c

portion of cured thermosetting polymer materialc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000267

A portion of solid material that is an output of a polymer curing process.
is equivalent to
portion of polymer-based material c and (output of op some thermosetting polymer curing process c)
has super-classes
portion of solid material c

portion of elastomer materialc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000250

A portion of polymer-based material that bears a disposition to be elastic (elasticity).
is equivalent to
portion of polymer-based material c and (has disposition op some elasticity c)
has super-classes
portion of polymer-based material c
is disjoint with
portion of plastic material c

portion of inorganic or hybrid polymer-based synthetic materialc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000130

A portion of synthetic polymer-based material that is composed of inorganic or hybrid polymer molecules.
has super-classes
portion of synthetic polymer-based material c

portion of materialc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.semanticweb.org/bionik/portion_of_material

The class is introduced for householding purposes, such that all "portion of" classes can be subsumed under it.
A portion of material is a material entity such that its identity depends only on the atoms or molecules present (whatever is the larger unit present), irrespective of size, shape, structure or kind membership of the whole portion.
has super-classes
material entity c, portion of matter c
has sub-classes
portion of polymer-based material c, portion of solid material c

portion of matterc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0000001

It is also representing a distinct physical or conceptual part of a material or substance. The open energy ontology OEO defines portion of matter as a subclass of object aggregate, which implies countability. Our portion is not (precisely) countable and thus bearer of intensive properties.
A material entity that is not demarcated by any physical discontinuities. At some finer level of granularity it is an object aggregate, at some coarser level of granularity it is a fiat object part,but at this level of granularity it is neither.
has super-classes
material entity c
has sub-classes
chemical substance c, material c, portion of material c

portion of natural plastic materialc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000249

A potion of plastic that is constituted of polymer molecules produced by processes whose agents do not include humans or their technologies.
has super-classes
portion of plastic material c
is disjoint with
portion of synthetic plastic material c

portion of plastic materialc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000247

a portion of polymer-based material that bears thermoplasticity or thermosetting dispositions, and not elasticity.
is equivalent to
portion of polymer-based material c and (not (has disposition op some elasticity c)) and (has disposition op some thermoplasticity c or thermosetting disposition c)
has super-classes
portion of polymer-based material c
has sub-classes
portion of biodegradable plastic material c, portion of natural plastic material c
is disjoint with
portion of elastomer material c

portion of polyethylenec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000293

A portion of thermoplastic polymer-based material produced by polymerization of ethylene monomers including further crosslinking and modifications using other comonomers ( with exclusion of ultra-high molecular polyethylene).
has super-classes
portion of synthetic polymer-based material c, portion of thermoplastic polymer-based material c
has sub-classes
high-density polyethylene c, linear low-density polyethylene c, low-density polyethylene c

portion of polymer composite-based synthetic materialc back to ToC or Class ToC

A portion of synthetic polymer-based material that incorporates non-polymeric molecules as reinforcement within the polymer matrix.
has super-classes
portion of synthetic polymer-based material c

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000246

A portion of material that is composed of polymer molecules. It may also contain further molecules, such as additives.
has super-classes
portion of material c
has sub-classes
portion of elastomer material c, portion of plastic material c, portion of synthetic polymer-based material c, portion of thermoplastic polymer-based material c, portion of thermosetting polymer-based material c

portion of synthetic plastic material c

A portion of synthetic plastic material that is composed of recycled polymer molecules.

portion of synthetic plastic material c
is disjoint with
portion of virgin plastic material c

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000244

A portion of material that presents a solid state.
is equivalent to
portion of material c and (has quality op some quality of a solid c)

portion of material c

portion of amorphous solid material c, portion of cured thermosetting polymer material c

A portion of plastic material that is composed of polymer molecules that are both synthetic and biodegradable.
has super-classes
portion of biodegradable plastic material c
has sub-classes
portion of compostable synthetic plastic material c

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000248

A portion of plastic that is composed of some synthetic polymer molecules.
has super-classes
portion of synthetic polymer-based material c
has sub-classes
portion of bio-based plastic material c, portion of recyclable plastic material c, portion of recycled plastic material c, portion of virgin plastic material c
is disjoint with
portion of natural plastic material c

portion of synthetic polymer-based materialc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000211

A portion of polymer-based material that in which some synthetic polymer molecules constitute the material matrix (frame).
has super-classes
portion of polymer-based material c
has sub-classes
portion of copolymer-based synthetic material c, portion of inorganic or hybrid polymer-based synthetic material c, portion of polyethylene c, portion of polymer composite-based synthetic material c, portion of synthetic plastic material c, portion of thermoplastic elastomer-based synthetic material c

portion of thermoplastic elastomer-based synthetic materialc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000216

A portion of synthetic polymer-based material that bears a disposition to be elastic (elasticity) and a dispostion to be thermoplastic (thermoplasticity).
is equivalent to
portion of synthetic polymer-based material c and ((has disposition op some thermoplasticity c) and (has disposition op some elasticity c))
has super-classes
portion of synthetic polymer-based material c

A portion of polymer-based material that bears a disposition to be thermoplastic (thermoplasticity).
is equivalent to
portion of polymer-based material c and (has disposition op some thermoplasticity c)
has super-classes
portion of polymer-based material c
has sub-classes
portion of polyethylene c

is equivalent to

has super-classes

A portion of plastic material that is composed by polymer molecules that have not been recycled.
has super-classes

is disjoint with
