positionc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PATO_0000140

A spatial quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's spatial location relative to other objects in the vicinity.
has super-classes
physical quality c
has sub-classes
superficial c

powderc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0000893

A powder is a dry, solid disconnected material entity aggregate composed of many very fine particles. These particles can flow freely when shaken or tilted.
has super-classes
disconnected material entity aggregate c

primary microplastic particlec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ENVO_01000945

A microplastic particle which is the direct output of a manufacturing process.
Contributors:
https://orcid.org/0000-0002-2996-719X
https://orcid.org/0000-0002-8166-0486
https://orcid.org/0000-0003-1072-8081
has super-classes
microplastic particle c

primary microplastic particlec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000262

A microplastic debris particle that was, at the origin, intentionally designed and produced of this size (1-1000 µm).
has super-classes
microplastic particle - Hartmann et al. (2019) classification c

processc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000015

p is a process = Def. p is an occurrent that has temporal proper parts and for some time t, p s-depends_on some material entity at t. (axiom label in BFO2 Reference: [083-003])
(Elucidation) p is a process means p is an occurrent that has some temporal proper part and for some time t, p has some material entity as participant
Example 
An act of selling; the life of an organism; a process of sleeping; a process of cell-division; a beating of the heart; a process of meiosis; the taxiing of an aircraft; the programming of a computer
has super-classes
occurrent c
has sub-classes
biological process c, curing c, exposure process c, fragmentation process of a piece of polymer-based material c, history c, interaction c, mineralization process c, planned process c, plastic combustion process c, plastic pollution process c, polymer degradation process c, polymerization c, system development c, transportation process c, weathering process of a piece of synthetic polymer-based material c
is in domain of
concretizes op, concretizes at all times op, has agent at time t op, has input op, has participant op, has participant at all times op, occupies spatiotemporal region op, occupies temporal region op, occurs in op, realizes op
is in range of
agent in at time t op, environs op, has realization op, is concretized by op, is concretized by at all times op, participates in op, participates in at all times op
is disjoint with
temporal region c, spatiotemporal region c, process boundary c

process boundaryc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000035

p is a process boundary =Def p is a temporal part of a process & p has no proper temporal parts
Example 
The boundary between the 2nd and 3rd year of your life
has super-classes
occurrent c
is in domain of
occupies spatiotemporal region op, occupies temporal region op, occurs in op
is in range of
environs op
is disjoint with
temporal region c, spatiotemporal region c, process c

pure substancec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CHEBI_60003

A pure substance is a chemical substance composed of multiple molecules, which are all of the same kind.
Example 
Pure water, a portion of iron atoms. In contrast, salt water 'has part' a portion of pure water and a portion of pure NaCl. Steel 'has part' a 'portion of pure iron', a 'portion of pure carbon' and possibly portions of other alloying- and impurity-elements.
has super-classes
chemical substance c

qualityc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000019

(Elucidation) A quality is a specifically dependent continuant that, in contrast to roles and dispositions, does not require any further process in order to be realized
Example 
The colour of a tomato; the ambient temperature of this portion of air; the length of the circumference of your waist; the shape of your nose; the shape of your nostril; the mass of this piece of gold
has super-classes
specifically dependent continuant c
has sub-classes
physical object quality c, relational quality c
is in range of
has quality op
is disjoint with
realizable entity c

qualityc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PATO_0000001

A dependent entity that inheres in a bearer by virtue of how the bearer is related to other entities
has super-classes
specifically dependent continuant c
has sub-classes
physical object quality c, quantitative c

quality of a fluidc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PATO_0080001

A physical quality inhering in an entity exhibiting the physical charachteristics of a material that flows under applied force.
has super-classes
quality of a substance c

quality of a gasc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PATO_0001547

A physical quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's exhibiting the physical characteristics of an entity consisting of particles that have neither a defined volume nor defined shape.
has super-classes
quality of a substance c

quality of a solidc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PATO_0001546

A physical quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's exhibiting the physical characteristics of an entity characterized by particles arranged such that their shape and volume are relatively stable.
has super-classes
quality of a substance c

quality of a substancec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PATO_0002198

A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of its constitution.
has super-classes
physical quality c
has sub-classes
quality of a fluid c, quality of a gas c, quality of a solid c

quantitativec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PATO_0103000

A quality of an entity that can be represented numerically, including anything that can be counted, measured, or given a numerical value.
has super-classes
quality c

realizable entityc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000017

A specifically dependent continuant that inheres in continuant entities and are not exhibited in full at every time in which it inheres in an entity or group of entities. The exhibition or actualization of a realizable entity is a particular manifestation, functioning or process that occurs under certain circumstances.
(Elucidation) A realizable entity is a specifically dependent continuant that inheres in some independent continuant which is not a spatial region & which is of a type some instances of which are realized in processes of a correlated type
Example 
The role of being a doctor; the role of this boundary to delineate where Utah and Colorado meet; the function of your reproductive organs; the disposition of your blood to coagulate; the disposition of this piece of metal to conduct electricity
has super-classes
specifically dependent continuant c
has sub-classes
disposition c, plan c, role c, role c
is in domain of
has realization op
is in range of
realizes op
is disjoint with
quality c

recyclabilityc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000188

A disposition that is realised in a waste recycling process that is cost-effective (primarily in terms of energy and value spent and generated).
has super-classes
disposition c

recyclable plastic manufacturing processc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000193

A plastic manufacturing process that has some recyclable plastic polymer molecules as the main input and some portiono of recyclable plastic as the main output.
is equivalent to
plastic manufacturing process c and (has primary output op some portion of recyclable plastic material c)
has super-classes
plastic manufacturing process c

recyclable plastic objectc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000192

A plastic object that is primarily made of recyclable plastic.
has super-classes

recyclable polymer moleculec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000194

A synthetic polymer molecule that bears a disposition to be recycled (recyclability).
is equivalent to
synthetic polymer molecule c and (has disposition op some recyclability c)
has super-classes
synthetic polymer molecule c

recycled plastic manufacturing processc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000199

A plastic manufacturing process that has some recycled plastic polymer molecules as the main input and some portion of recycled plastic as the main output.
is equivalent to
plastic manufacturing process c and (has primary output op some portion of recycled plastic material c)
has super-classes
plastic manufacturing process c

recycled plastic objectc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000198

A plastic object that is primarily made of recycled plastic.
has super-classes

recycled polymer moleculec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000200

A synthetic polymer molecule that is the main output of a recycling process of synthetic polymer molecule.
is equivalent to
synthetic polymer molecule c and (output of op some recycling process of synthetic polymer molecules c)

recycling process of synthetic polymer moleculesc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000201

Reference to specific types of recycling processes, such as chemical recycling and mechanical recycling, can be added as needed.
A material processing that leads to the recovery of recyclable polymer molecules from waste pieces of synthetic polymer-based material.
has super-classes

Redc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.ontologyrepository.com/CommonCoreOntologies/Red

A Color that is above Orange with a wavelength in the visible spectrum typically between 635 to 700 nanometers.
has super-classes
Color c

relational qualityc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000145

b is a relational quality =Def b is a quality & there exists c and d such that c and d are not identical & b specifically depends on c & b specifically depends on d
Example 
A marriage bond; an instance of love; an obligation between one person and another
has super-classes
quality c

release process of plastic debris-carried pollutants into the environmentc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000202

Implementations may revise this class axiomatization with reference to more adequate and specific object properties to represent variations of levels of plastic debris presence. Revisions will primarily target the object property “increased levels of”. Also, see comments to the object property “increased levels of”. http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ENVO_02000200
A transportation process that has some portion of synthetic polymer-based material (generally synthetic plastic) composing some plastic debris and having some pollutants as part, as participant, and, as output, 1) that portion of synthetic polymer-based material with a decreased level of those pollutants as part, and 2) some other portion(s) of material with an increased level of those pollutants (both compared to levels prior participating into that process).
has super-classes
transportation process c

Representational Information Content Entityc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.ontologyrepository.com/CommonCoreOntologies/RepresentationalInformationContentEntity

is equivalent to
Information Content Entity c and (represents op some entity c)
has super-classes
Information Content Entity c
has sub-classes
indicator value c
is in domain of
represents op
is in range of
represented by op
is disjoint with
Measurement Information Content Entity c, perspective information content entity c, worth information content entity c

residue molecule of synthetic plastic manufacturingc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000203

An upper-level class concerning residues included in polymer materials other than plastics can be added as needed.
A molecule that is a waste of some plastic manufacturing process and is incorporated into some portion of synthetic plastic material.
has super-classes
molecular entity c

resiliencec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.unep.org/sdg/SDGIO_00010021

A disposition of an entity to recover its previous composition, qualities, and functionality following some perturbation.
This disposition is distinct from robustness, which pertains to an entity's ability to resist change when encountering perturbing forces.
Term status
http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000125
has super-classes
disposition c

rigid polyvinyl chloridec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0050014

polyvinyl chloride that is characterized by its stiffness and durability
has super-classes
polyvinyl chloride c

riskc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.unep.org/sdg/SDGIO_00000020

A disposition of a bearer to realise one or more of its vulnerabilities when participating in a process which exposes the bearer to a hazard or combination of hazards.
Risk is increased when harm or damage is realised and when an entity is likely to encounter a hazard in some process. The probability of this disposition being realised can be assigned on the information or data level.
Term status
http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000125
has super-classes
disposition c

rolec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000023

A realizable entity the manifestation of which brings about some result or end that is not essential to a continuant in virtue of the kind of thing that it is but that can be served or participated in by that kind of continuant in some kinds of natural, social or institutional contexts.
(Elucidation) A role b is a realizable entity such that b exists because there is some single bearer that is in some special physical, social, or institutional set of circumstances in which this bearer does not have to be & b is not such that, if it ceases to exist, then the physical make-up of the bearer is thereby changed
Example 
The priest role; the student role; the role of subject in a clinical trial; the role of a stone in marking a property boundary; the role of a boundary to demarcate two neighbouring administrative territories; the role of a building in serving as a military target
has super-classes
realizable entity c
has sub-classes
device role c, indicator-role c, litter role c, plastic debris sink role c, plastic debris source role c, stakeholder-role c, synthetic polymer-based material additive role c, waste role c, worth-role c
is in range of
has role op
is disjoint with
disposition c

rolec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CHEBI_50906

A role is particular behaviour which a material entity may exhibit.
has super-classes
realizable entity c
has sub-classes
biological role c, chemical role c

scanning electron microscopec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0000922

A scanning electron microscope (SEM) is a measuring device that produces high-resolution images of a sample surface by scanning it with a focused beam of electrons.
has super-classes
electron microscope c

secondary microplastic particlec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ENVO_01000946

A microplastic particle which is derived from the breakdown of larger plastic debris.
The debris that make up the Great Pacific Garbage Patch constantly release microplastics as they break down.
Contributors:
https://orcid.org/0000-0002-2996-719X
https://orcid.org/0000-0002-8166-0486
https://orcid.org/0000-0003-1072-8081
has super-classes
microplastic particle c

secondary microplastic particlec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000260

A microplastic debris particle that derives from a fragmentation process occurring in an environment (no human intention involved) or results from the usage of some items made of polymer-based materials.
has super-classes
microplastic particle - Hartmann et al. (2019) classification c

self-organizationc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/sco#SCO_0000043

This class should be amended according to SCO implementations regarding system scales, levels, and change.
A quality that inherits in a complex system by virtue of how the complex system components spontaneously arrange, globally generating macroscopic and microscopic structures and behaviors, without any internal and/or external controls.
has super-classes
complex system property c
is disjoint with
emergence c

setting datumc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000140

A settings datum is a datum that denotes some configuration of an instrument.
Term status
http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000123
has super-classes
data item c

shapec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PATO_0000052

A morphological quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's ratios of distances between its features (points, edges, surfaces and also holes etc).
Shapes are invariant on size transformations. Shapes can be subdivided into 2D and 3D shapes, We can also make a distinction between shapes of complete self-connected objects, and shapes of parts of objects.
has super-classes
morphology c
has sub-classes
2-D shape c, 3-D shape c, irregularly shaped c, linear c

silicone rubberc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0090052

synthetic rubber that belongs to crosslinked polysiloxane elastomer family, with organic side groups
Example 
widely used for its broad service temperature range in medical devices, gaskets, and culinary tools
has super-classes
synthetic rubber c

sitec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000029

(Elucidation) A site is a three-dimensional immaterial entity whose boundaries either (partially or wholly) coincide with the boundaries of one or more material entities or have locations determined in relation to some material entity
Example 
A hole in a portion of cheese; a rabbit hole; the Grand Canyon; the Piazza San Marco; the kangaroo-joey-containing hole of a kangaroo pouch; your left nostril (a fiat part - the opening - of your left nasal cavity); the lumen of your gut; the hold of a ship; the interior of the trunk of your car; hole in an engineered floor joist
has super-classes
immaterial entity c
is in domain of
environs op
is in range of
occurs in op
is disjoint with
spatial region c, continuant fiat boundary c

sizec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PATO_0000117

A morphology quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's physical magnitude.
has super-classes
morphology c
has sub-classes
1-D extent c, 2-D extent c, 3-D extent c

spatial regionc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000006

(Elucidation) A spatial region is a continuant entity that is a continuant part of the spatial projection of a portion of spacetime at a given time
Example 
As for zero-dimensional spatial region, one-dimensional spatial region, two-dimensional spatial region, three-dimensional spatial region
has super-classes
immaterial entity c
has sub-classes
one-dimensional spatial region c, three-dimensional spatial region c, two-dimensional spatial region c, zero-dimensional spatial region c
is in range of
occupies spatial region op, occupies spatial region at all times op, spatially projects onto op, spatially projects onto at all times op
is disjoint with
site c, continuant fiat boundary c

spatiotemporal regionc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000011

(Elucidation) A spatiotemporal region is an occurrent that is an occurrent part of spacetime
Example 
The spatiotemporal region occupied by the development of a cancer tumour; the spatiotemporal region occupied by an orbiting satellite
has super-classes
occurrent c
is in domain of
spatially projects onto op, spatially projects onto at all times op, temporally projects onto op
is in range of
occupies spatiotemporal region op
is disjoint with
temporal region c, process c, process boundary c

specifically dependent continuantc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000020

b is a relational specifically dependent continuant = Def. b is a specifically dependent continuant and there are n &gt; 1 independent continuants c1, … cn which are not spatial regions are such that for all 1 i &lt; j n, ci and cj share no common parts, are such that for each 1 i n, b s-depends_on ci at every time t during the course of b’s existence (axiom label in BFO2 Reference: [131-004])
b is a specifically dependent continuant =Def b is a continuant & there is some independent continuant c which is not a spatial region & which is such that b specifically depends on c
Example 
(with multiple bearers) John's love for Mary; the ownership relation between John and this statue; the relation of authority between John and his subordinates
(with one bearer) The mass of this tomato; the pink colour of a medium rare piece of grilled filet mignon at its centre; the smell of this portion of mozzarella; the disposition of this fish to decay; the role of being a doctor; the function of this heart to pump blood; the shape of this hole
has super-classes
continuant c
has sub-classes
complex system property c, quality c, quality c, realizable entity c
is in domain of
agent in at time t op, concretizes op, concretizes at all times op, inheres in op, participates in op, participates in at all times op, specifically depended on by op, specifically depends on op
is in range of
bearer of op, has agent at time t op, has characteristic op, has participant op, has participant at all times op, is concretized by op, is concretized by at all times op, specifically depended on by op, specifically depends on op
is disjoint with
independent continuant c, generically dependent continuant c

specification datumc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0060009

The intent of the specification datum is to express the "Sollwert" of some qualitiy or behavioral material property. Then, the specification datum can be further specified by some value specification.
A directive information entity that provides information of a setpoint of some value, i.e., it is an intended value.
Example 
The setpoint value of a ultimate tensile strength of some steel material
has super-classes
directive information entity c

sphericalc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PATO_0001499

A spheroid quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's resembling a ball (a sphere whose equatorial diameter is equal to the polar diameter).
has super-classes
spheroid c

spheroidc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PATO_0001865

A convex 3-D shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having a quadric surface in three dimensions obtained by rotating an ellipse about one of its principal axes. Includes spheres and oblate/prolate spheroids.
has super-classes
convex 3-D shape c
has sub-classes
spherical c

stakeholderc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/sco#SCO_0000036

A complex system component with a stakeholder role.
is equivalent to
complex system component c and (has role op some stakeholder-role c)
has super-classes
complex system component c

stakeholder perspectivec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/sco#SCO_0000030

A perspective that is bearered by a stakeholder.
is equivalent to
perspective c and (disposition of op some stakeholder c)
has super-classes
perspective c

stakeholder perspective information content entityc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/sco#SCO_0000040

A perspective information content entity of a stakeholder perspective.
is equivalent to
perspective information content entity c and (is about op some stakeholder perspective c)
has super-classes
perspective information content entity c

stakeholder-rolec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/sco#SCO_0000018

A role whose realization leads the complex system component to be recognized as a bearer, in the belonging complex system, of at least one role (different from the role of stakeholder) and/or disposition (primarily a disposition to benefit/be damaged as being part of that complex system).
has super-classes
role c

structurec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PATO_0000141

A morphology quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's relative position, shape, arrangements and connectivity of an organism's various parts; the pattern underlying its form.
has super-classes
morphology c
has sub-classes
unstructured c

synthetic rubber and belongs to a family of synthetic random copolymer elastomers of styrene and butadiene, made by emulsion or solution polymerization and typically vulcanized
has super-classes
synthetic rubber c

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PATO_0002226

nanoplastic particle - Hartmann et al. (2019) classification c

IRI: https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0000963

A chromatography system for separating and analyzing compounds using supercritical fluids as the mobile phase in chromatography.

chromatography system c

superellipticc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PATO_0002318

A shape constituting a transition between a rectangle and a circle; a closed curve, of which the circle and ellipse are special cases, whose parametric equation is x = a.cos2/rt, y = b.cos2/rt
has super-classes
2-D shape c

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PATO_0001665

A spatial pattern inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being located near a surface.
has super-classes
position c

polypropylene in which the methyl groups alternate regularly along the polymer chain
has super-classes

plastic biomineralization process c and (has input op some (biodegradable polymer molecule c and synthetic polymer molecule c) and (continuant part of at all times op some portion of synthetic biodegradable plastic material c))

synthetic plastic degradation processc back to ToC or Class ToC

is equivalent to
plastic degradation process c and (has input op some synthetic polymer molecule c and (continuant part of at all times op some portion of synthetic plastic material c))

synthetic plastic fragmentation processc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000208

fragmentation process of a piece of synthetic polymer-based material c and (has input op some piece of synthetic plastic c) and (has output op some plastic fragment c)

synthetic polymerc back to ToC or Class ToC

A polymer which is the output of a manufacturing process.

Authors:

is equivalent to

synthetic polymer moleculec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000210

References to natural polymer molecules and related production processes can be added as needed.
A polymer molecule that is a output of a synthetic polymerization process.
is equivalent to
polymer molecule c and (output of op some synthetic polymerization process c)
has super-classes
polymer molecule c
has sub-classes
bio-based polymer molecule c, modified natural polymer molecule c, recyclable polymer molecule c

synthetic polymer-based material additivec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000212

Here, we aim to track molecules that have already been added to polymers to obtain (plastic) polymer materials, not molecules that will be added. Currently, the ClimOO ontology does not intend such molecules to be already additive. Revisions can implement the ClimOO Ontology representation in this sense.
A molecule with a synthetic polymer-based material additive role.
has super-classes
molecular entity c
has sub-classes
plastic additive c

synthetic polymer-based material additive rolec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000213

A role that is realized in a molecule when it is added to some synthetic polymers to obtain a synthetic polymer-based material whose properties are modified (generally, enhanced) to fulfill the purposes of some stakeholders. Considering plastics, examples of additives include plasticizers, stabilizers, flame retardants, flow modifiers, processing aids, impact modifiers, antioxidants, pigments, biocides, and fragrances.
Source
References to additive incorporation, such as chemical binding or juxtaposition, can be added as needed.
http://purl.allotrope.org/ontologies/role#AFRL_0000216
has super-classes
role c

synthetic polymer-based material degradation processc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000274

An process that leads some synthetic polymer molecules constituting some portion of synthetic polymer-bsed material to vary their physical and chemical qualities due to interactions with factors such as heat, light, or chemical entities, resulting in a decrease in their molecular weight.
is equivalent to
polymer degradation process c and (has input op some synthetic polymer molecule c and (continuant part of at all times op some portion of synthetic polymer-based material c))
has super-classes
polymer degradation process c
has sub-classes
degradation process of a piece of synthetic polymer-based material c, synthetic plastic degradation process c

synthetic polymer-based material manufacturing processc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000276

A material processing that has a primary output a portion of synthetic polymer-based material.
has super-classes
material processing c
has sub-classes
plastic manufacturing process c

synthetic polymer-based material sorption process of pollutantsc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000214

Reference to specific types of sorption, such as absorption and adsorption, can be added as needed.
A material transport process that has as output some portion of synthetic polymer-based material with an increased level of pollutants as part (compared to levels prior to participating in that process). Pollutants can both penetrate the material matrix (absorption) or remain confined on its surface (adsorption). This process generally concerns plastic materials composing plastic debris and litter.
Source
Fu, L., Li, J., Wang, G., Luan, Y., & Dai, W. (2021). Adsorption behavior of organic pollutants on microplastics. Ecotoxicology and Environmental Safety, 217, 112207. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.ecoenv.2021.112207
has super-classes
transportation process c
has sub-classes
plastic debris sorption process of pollutants c, plastic sorption process of pollutants c

synthetic polymerization processc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000290

A polymerization process that is human-driven and has some synthetic polymer molecules as output.
is equivalent to
polymerization process c and (has primary output op some synthetic polymer molecule c)
has super-classes
polymerization process c

synthetic rubberc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0050032

elastomer that is produced through chemical synthesis to mimic natural rubber’s properties
has super-classes
elastomer c
has sub-classes
ethylene propylene diene monomer c, nitrile butadiene rubber c, polychloropren c, silicone rubber c, styrene-butadiene rubber c

systemc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002577

A material entity consisting of multiple components that are causally integrated.
SCO cretor comment: RO defines the class “system” as a subclass of the “material entity” class. Nevertheless, a system is a fiat object, e.g. an object that is “delimited” according to the perspective of the stakeholder (Landgrebe and Smith 2022, p. 117,118). System delimitation determines the boundaries of the systems, but the stakeholder, however, does not create the system through this delimitation process (Landgrebe and Smith 2022, p. 117,118). As this class is extensively reused among ontologies within the OBO-Foundry context, we maintain its current hierarchical position. However, we recommend a revision of this class. There are two possible strategies to consider: 1) to reclassify this class as a subclass of “fiat object part” one or 2) to create a dedicated “fiat object” class that will be a subclass of “material entity” and the superclass for the “system” one (and related subclasses). We leave the discussion open with the intention of engaging with subject matter experts, planning that these modifications may be included in the next release of this resource. As the environment of a system corresponds to what has not been delimited as part of such a system, these considerations correspondingly also apply to the class “system environment” (at least regarding the system-system environment boundaries).
Source
http://www.jbiomedsem.com/content/4/1/43
has super-classes
material entity c
has sub-classes
complex system c
is disjoint with
system environment c

system agentc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/sco#SCO_0000029

We envision revisions of the current SCO theory of agency. This is due to the limited power of explainability of present representation. A sounder and deeper interpretation of the causal role of an agent in an interaction, compared to other participants, is required. Tentative proposal: a possible direction of implementation is towards a telic vision of agency intended as end-point driven. On this more fundamental neutral vision, a second layer of representation may be constructed to model intentionality, goals, and plans in relation to agency. Here, SCO may harmonize and align with UFO-C representation. For insight for discussion see: Guarino, N., & Guizzardi, G. (2024). Processes as variable embodiments. Synthese, 203(4), 104. https://doi.org/10.1007/s11229-024-04505-2 Guizzardi, G., de Almeida Falbo, R., & Guizzardi, R. (2008). Grounding software domain ontologies in the unified foundational ontology (UFO): The case of the ODE. Memorias de la XI Conferencia Iberoamericana de Software Engineering (CIbSE 2008), Recife, Pernambuco, Brasil, February 13-17, 2008, 127–140.
A system component that is an agent in an interaction among system components or in an interaction between system components and system environment components.
is equivalent to
system component c and (agent in at time t op some interaction among system components c or interaction between system components and system environment components c) and (agent in at time t op only interaction among system components c or interaction between system components and system environment components c)
has super-classes
system component c
has sub-classes
complex system agent c
is disjoint with
system environnement agent c

system boundaryc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/sco#SCO_0000024

A fiat surface of a system.
is equivalent to
fiat surface c and (proper continuant part of at all times op some system c)
has super-classes
fiat surface c
has sub-classes
complex system boundary c

system componentc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/sco#SCO_0000013

Despite minor differences, SCO representation of systems and related architecture and composition is compatible with that of the System Core Ontology (Calhau et al., 2024). The system Core Ontology centers on socio-techno systems, primarily pointing to an intention/goal-directed representation of these systems. SCO representation, instead, adopts a more neutral modeling approach for complex systems, seeking to account for a wider range of systems. SCO is also more compact than the system Core Ontology. This is, however, adequate for the current purposes of SCO. We envision employing the system Core Ontology as a starting point to extend SCO representation of system architecture, situations, properties, and functions. We also envision possible alignments and reciprocal implementations between these two ontologies. Calhau, R. F., Prince Sales, T., Oliveira, Í., Kokkula, S., Ferreira Pires, L., Cameron, D., Guizzardi, G., & Almeida, J. P. A. (2024). A System Core Ontology for Capability Emergence Modeling. In H. A. Proper, L. Pufahl, D. Karastoyanova, M. Van Sinderen, & J. Moreira (A c. Di), Enterprise Design, Operations, and Computing (Vol. 14367, pp. 3–20). Springer Nature Switzerland. https://doi.org/10.1007/978-3-031-46587-1_1 Ubbiali, G. A., Borghini, A., & Lange, M. C. (2024). Ontologies for Sustainability: Theoretical Challenges. https://doi.org/10.31219/osf.io/z8uqr
A material entity that belongs to a system.
is equivalent to
material entity c and (continuant part of op some system c)
has super-classes
material entity c
has sub-classes
complex system component c, system agent c
is disjoint with
system environment component c

system developmentc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/sco#SCO_0000002

SCO must extend its representation to (complex) system behaviors. A possible starting point may be the work of Calhau et al. (2023) and Calhau et al. (2024). Calhau, R., Sales, T. P., Almeida, J. P. A., & Guizzardi, G. (2023). Exploring system behavior in a system ontology. Int Conf Concept Model. Calhau, R. F., Prince Sales, T., Oliveira, Í., Kokkula, S., Ferreira Pires, L., Cameron, D., Guizzardi, G., & Almeida, J. P. A. (2024). A System Core Ontology for Capability Emergence Modeling. In H. A. Proper, L. Pufahl, D. Karastoyanova, M. Van Sinderen, & J. Moreira (A c. Di), Enterprise Design, Operations, and Computing (Vol. 14367, pp. 3–20). Springer Nature Switzerland. https://doi.org/10.1007/978-3-031-46587-1_1
A process whose specific outcome is the progression of a system over time from an initial condition to a later condition.
has super-classes
process c
has sub-classes
complex system development c
is disjoint with
system history c

system environment componentc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/sco#SCO_0000014

We envision discussions with subject matter experts regarding whether the current SCO representation of system-system component (and related environment) parthood is suitable or whether a proper parthood-based representation is more adequate. Insights for discussion may be found in Calhau et al. (2024) and Ubbiali et al. (2024). Calhau, R. F., Prince Sales, T., Oliveira, Í., Kokkula, S., Ferreira Pires, L., Cameron, D., Guizzardi, G., & Almeida, J. P. A. (2024). A System Core Ontology for Capability Emergence Modeling. In H. A. We envision revisions of the current SCO theory of agency. This is due to the limited power of explainability of present representation. A sounder and deeper interpretation of the causal role of an agent in an interaction, compared to other participants, is required. Tentative proposal: a possible direction of implementation is towards a telic vision of agency intended as end-point driven. On this more fundamental neutral vision, a second layer of representation may be constructed to model intentionality, goals, and plans in relation to agency. Here, SCO may harmonize and align with UFO-C representation. For insight for discussion see: Guarino, N., & Guizzardi, G. (2024). Processes as variable embodiments. polymer molecule c and (continuant part of at all times op some portion of polymer-based material c and (has disposition op some thermoplasticity c))
has super-classes
polymer molecule c

thermoplasticityc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ENVO_06105002

A disposition inhering in a bearer by virtue of that bearer's ability to, repeatedly, become plastic on heating and harden again on cooling.
ClimOO Ontology developer comment: References to processes realizing this disposition can be added as needed.
Date:
27T09:10:14Z/04/2021
Authors:
https://orcid.org/0000-0002-8166-0486
Contributors:
https://orcid.org/0000-0002-2996-719X
https://orcid.org/0000-0003-1072-8081
has super-classes
disposition c
is disjoint with
elasticity c

thermosetting dispositionc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ENVO_06105004

A disposition which inheres in materials capable of becoming rigid when cured by heating.
ClimOO Ontology developer comment: References to processes realizing this disposition can be added as needed.
Date:
27T13:46:52Z/04/2021
Authors:
https://orcid.org/0000-0002-8166-0486

https://orcid.org/0000-0002-2996-719X
https://orcid.org/0000-0003-1072-8081
has super-classes
disposition c
is disjoint with
elasticity c

thermosetting polymerc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0050022

polymer that, once cured, irreversibly sets into a permanent shape
is equivalent to
portion of thermosetting polymer-based material c
has super-classes
polymer c
has sub-classes
epoxy resin c, melamine formaldehyde c, phenolic resin c, thermosetting polyurethane c, unsaturated polyester resins c, urea formaldehyde c

thermosetting polymer curing processc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000268

The toughening or hardening of a portion of thermosetting polymer material by cross-linking of polymer molecule chains. This process has a portion of thermosetting polymer as input and a portion of cured thermosetting polymer as output.
Source
https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0010034
is equivalent to
process c and (realizes op some thermosetting disposition c) and (has input op some portion of thermosetting polymer-based material c) and (has output op some portion of cured thermosetting polymer material c)
has super-classes
curing c

thermosetting polymer moleculec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000218

A polymer molecule that is part of some portion of polymer-based material having a disposition to be thermoset (thermosetting disposition).
is equivalent to
polymer molecule c and (continuant part of at all times op some portion of polymer-based material c and (has disposition op some thermosetting disposition c))
has super-classes
polymer molecule c

thermosetting polyurethanec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0090050

thermosetting polymer with carbamate crosslinks
has super-classes
thermosetting polymer c

thin layer chromatography systemc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0000990

A chromatography system used for separating compounds in a sample using a thin layer of adsorbent material on a plate.
has super-classes
chromatography system c

three-dimensional spatial regionc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000028

(Elucidation) A three-dimensional spatial region is a whole consisting of a spatial volume together with zero or more spatial volumes which may have spatial regions of lower dimension as parts
Example 
A cube-shaped region of space; a sphere-shaped region of space; the region of space occupied by all and only the planets in the solar system at some point in time
has super-classes
spatial region c
has sub-classes
system spatial region c
is disjoint with
two-dimensional spatial region c, zero-dimensional spatial region c, one-dimensional spatial region c

time unitc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UO_0000003

A unit which is a standard measure of the dimension in which events occur in sequence.
has super-classes
unit c

tire wear and road particlec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000219

A tire wear particle that is aggregated with some road particles.
has super-classes
tire wear particle c

tire wear particlec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000220

A debris particle of polymer composite-based synthetic material that is an output of the abrasion process of vehicle tires during driving (vehicle motion).
has super-classes
plastic debris particle c
has sub-classes
tire wear and road particle c

transmission electron microscopec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0025008

An electron microscope that produces high-resolution images of a sample's internal structure by transmitting electrons through the sample.
has super-classes
electron microscope c

transparentc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000229

For insights on definitions, see, for instance: https://www.merriam-webster.com/dictionary/transparent https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Transparency_and_translucency
has super-classes
Color c

transportation processc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000265

According to EnvO, we consider this class as provisional. We envision implementations.
A process during which material entities are displaced from a location and moved either to a new location or back to the original location.
has super-classes
process c
has sub-classes
planned transportation process of a piece of synthetic polymer-based material c, planned transportation process of a portion of synthetic polymer-based material c, release process of plastic debris-carried pollutants into the environment c, synthetic polymer-based material sorption process of pollutants c

transportation process of a plastic debris particlec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000291

A transportation process that concerns a plastic debris particle.
has super-classes
planned transportation process of a piece of synthetic plastic c

transportation process of a plastic objectc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000286

A transportation process that concerns a piece of synthetic plastic.
has super-classes
planned transportation process of a piece of synthetic plastic c

two-dimensional spatial regionc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000009

(Elucidation) A two-dimensional spatial region is a spatial region that is a whole consisting of a surface together with zero or more surfaces which may have spatial regions of lower dimension as parts
Example 
The surface of a sphere-shaped part of space; an infinitely thin plane in space
has super-classes
spatial region c
is disjoint with
zero-dimensional spatial region c, one-dimensional spatial region c, three-dimensional spatial region c

unitc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UO_0000000

A unit of measurement is a standardized quantity of a physical quality.
has super-classes
measurement unit label c
has sub-classes
mass percentage based unit c, mass volume percentage based unit c, mole fraction based unit c, time unit c

unsaturated polyester resinsc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0090051

thermosetting polymer system of unsaturated polyester prepolymers in reactive vinyl monomers; it cures by radical crosslinking into rigid networks
has super-classes
thermosetting polymer c

unstructuredc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PATO_0001412

A structural quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's lacking distinct structure.
has super-classes
structure c

urea formaldehydec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://w3id.org/pmd/co/PMD_0050029

thermosetting polymer formed from urea and formaldehyde, commonly used in adhesives and molding compounds.
has super-classes
thermosetting polymer c

usage process of some piece of synthetic plasticc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000292

A planned process that involves some piece of synthetic polymer-based material employed for some purpose.
has super-classes
usage process of some piece of synthetic polymer-based material c
has sub-classes
plastic object usage process c

usage process of some piece of synthetic polymer-based materialc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000221

A planned process that involves some piece of synthetic polymer-based material employed for some purpose.
has super-classes
completely executed planned process c
has sub-classes
usage process of some piece of synthetic plastic c

virgin plastic objectc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000223

A plastic object that is primarily made of virgin plastic.
has super-classes

volumec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PATO_0000918

A 3-D extent quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's amount of 3-dimensional space it occupies.
has super-classes
3-D extent c

vulnerabilityc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.unep.org/sdg/SDGIO_00000024

A disposition of an entity to undergo undesirable change in response to some exposure.
Term status
http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000428
has super-classes
disposition c

waste rolec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ENVO_01000665

A role that is realized in some process wherein the bearer is discarded or not utilized further.
has super-classes
role c

water solubilityc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000224

A disposition that realizes in a process of dissolving in water.
has super-classes
disposition c

weathering process of a piece of synthetic polymer-based materialc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/climoo/climoo-plastico#ClimOO_0000295

A process driven by natural forces that applies to a piece of synthetic polymer-based material and results in changing appearance or mechanical properties of that piece.
Source
http://dx.doi.org/10.1351/PAC-REC-10-12-04
has super-classes
process c
has sub-classes
plastic debris weathering c, plastic weathering c

Whitec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.ontologyrepository.com/CommonCoreOntologies/White

A Color of maximum brightness, the color of objects that reflect nearly all wavelengths of the visible light spectrum, thus considered achromatic.
has super-classes
Color c

worth information content entityc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/sco#SCO_0000005

An information content entity that generically depends on a complex system or a complex system environment (or some part(s) of them) and is about the worth (that concretizes it) that some complex system component or complex system environment component recognizes that that complex system or complex system environment (or some part(s) of them) bearer.
is equivalent to
Information Content Entity c and (is about op some worth-role c)
has super-classes
Information Content Entity c
is disjoint with
Representational Information Content Entity c, perspective information content entity c

worth-rolec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/sco#SCO_0000019

Due to the SCO focus on complex systems, the worth-role class have been designed referring specifically to complex systems. Nevertheless, future implementations may broaden the space of application to also systems. Implementations may equally deepen this representation, detailing and elucidating the distinction and connection between worth and value(s).
A role whose realization leads the complex system or the complex system environment (or some part(s) of them) to be recognized to bearer relevance by some complex system component or complex system environment component according to its/their perspective.
has super-classes
role c

xenobioticc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CHEBI_35703

A xenobiotic (Greek, <em>xenos</em> "foreign"; <em>bios</em> "life") is a compound that is foreign to a living organism. Principal xenobiotics include: drugs, carcinogens and various compounds that have been introduced into the environment by artificial means.
has super-classes
biological role c

Yellowc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.ontologyrepository.com/CommonCoreOntologies/Yellow

A Color that is between Orange and Green with a wavelength in the visible spectrum typically between 560 to 590 nanometers.
has super-classes
Color c

zero-dimensional spatial regionc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000018

(Elucidation) A zero-dimensional spatial region is one or a collection of more than one spatially disjoint points in space
Example 
The spatial region occupied at some time instant by the North Pole
has super-classes
spatial region c
is disjoint with
two-dimensional spatial region c, one-dimensional spatial region c, three-dimensional spatial region c

zero-dimensional temporal regionc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000148

(Elucidation) A zero-dimensional temporal region is a temporal region that is a whole consisting of one or more separated temporal instants as parts
Example 
A temporal region that is occupied by a process boundary; the moment at which a finger is detached in an industrial accident
has super-classes
temporal region c
has sub-classes
temporal instant c
is disjoint with
one-dimensional temporal region c

2D boundary ofop back to ToC or Object Property ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002000

a relation between a 2D immaterial entity (the boundary) and a material entity, in which the boundary delimits the material entity
Example 
the surface of my skin is a 2D boundary of my body
has super-properties
mereotopologically related to op

achieves_planned_objectiveop back to ToC or Object Property ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000417

This relation obtains between a planned process and a objective specification when the criteria specified in the objective specification are met at the end of the planned process.
Source
PPPB branch derived
Term status
http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000120
Example 
A cell sorting process achieves the objective specification 'material separation objective'
has domain
completely executed planned process c
has range
objective specification c
is inverse of
objective_achieved_by op

acts on population ofop back to ToC or Object Property ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0012003

p acts on population of c iff c' is a collection, has members of type c, and p has participant c
has super-properties
has participant op

acts upstream ofop back to ToC or Object Property ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002263

c acts upstream of p if and only if c enables some f that is involved in p' and p' occurs chronologically before p, is not part of p, and affects the execution of p. c is a material entity and f, p, p' are processes.
Example 
A faulty traffic light (material entity) whose malfunctioning (a process) is causally upstream of a traffic collision (a process): the traffic light acts upstream of the collision.
has super-properties
acts upstream of or within op
has sub-properties
acts upstream of, negative effect op, acts upstream of, positive effect op, involved in regulation of op

acts upstream of or withinop back to ToC or Object Property ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002264

c acts upstream of or within p if c is enables f, and f is causally upstream of or within p. c is a material entity and p is an process.
Example 
A gene product that has some activity, where that activity may be a part of a pathway or upstream of the pathway.
has super-properties
causal agent in process op
has sub-properties
acts upstream of op, acts upstream of or within, negative effect op, acts upstream of or within, positive effect op, involved in or involved in regulation of op

acts upstream of or within, negative effectop back to ToC or Object Property ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0004033

has super-properties
acts upstream of or within op
has sub-properties
acts upstream of, negative effect op

acts upstream of or within, positive effectop back to ToC or Object Property ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0004032

has super-properties
acts upstream of or within op
has sub-properties
acts upstream of, positive effect op

acts upstream of, negative effectop back to ToC or Object Property ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0004035

c 'acts upstream of, negative effect' p if c is enables f, and f is causally upstream of p, and the direction of f is negative
has super-properties
acts upstream of op, acts upstream of or within, negative effect op

acts upstream of, positive effectop back to ToC or Object Property ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0004034

c 'acts upstream of, positive effect' p if c is enables f, and f is causally upstream of p, and the direction of f is positive
has super-properties
acts upstream of op, acts upstream of or within, positive effect op

adjacent toop back to ToC or Object Property ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002220

x adjacent to y if and only if x and y share a boundary.
Term status
http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000125
Example 
A caterpillar walking on the surface of a leaf is adjacent_to the leaf, if one of the caterpillar appendages is touching the leaf. In contrast, a butterfly flying close to a flower is not considered adjacent, unless there are any touching parts.
The epidermis layer of a vertebrate is adjacent to the dermis.
The plasma membrane of a cell is adjacent to the cytoplasm, and also to the cell lumen which the cytoplasm occupies.
The skin of the forelimb is adjacent to the skin of the torso if these are considered anatomical subdivisions with a defined border. Otherwise a relation such as continuous_with would be used.
has super-properties
spatially disjoint from op
has sub-properties
surrounded by op, surrounds op

agent in at time top back to ToC or Object Property ToC

IRI: https://www.w3id.org/sco#SCO_0000038

We envision revisions of the current SCO theory of agency. This is due to the limited power of explainability of present representation. A sounder and deeper interpretation of the causal role of an agent in an interaction, compared to other participants, is required. Tentative proposal: a possible direction of implementation is towards a telic vision of agency intended as end-point driven. On this more fundamental neutral vision, a second layer of representation may be constructed to model intentionality, goals, and plans in relation to agency. Here, SCO may harmonize and align with UFO-C representation. For insight for discussion see: Guarino, N., & Guizzardi, G. (2024). Processes as variable embodiments. Synthese, 203(4), 104. https://doi.org/10.1007/s11229-024-04505-2 Guizzardi, G., de Almeida Falbo, R., & Guizzardi, R. (2008). Grounding software domain ontologies in the unified foundational ontology (UFO): The case of the ODE. Memorias de la XI Conferencia Iberoamericana de Software Engineering (CIbSE 2008), Recife, Pernambuco, Brasil, February 13-17, 2008, 127–140.
A primitive relation between a continuant x, a process y, and a time at which x is causally active in y.
has equivalent properties
causal agent in process op
has super-properties
participates in op
has domain
specifically dependent continuant c or generically dependent continuant c or (independent continuant c and (not (spatial region c)))
has range
process c
is inverse of
has agent at time t op

bearer ofop back to ToC or Object Property ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000196

b bearer of c =Def c inheres in b
Example 
A patch of ink is the bearer of a colour quality; an organism is the bearer of a temperature quality
has super-properties
specifically depended on by op
has domain
independent continuant c and (not (spatial region c))
has range
specifically dependent continuant c
is inverse of
inheres in op

beforeop back to ToC or Object Property ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002083

t1 before t2 iff:= t1 before_or_simulataneous_with t2 and not (t1 simultaeous_with t2)

has characteristics: transitive

has super-properties
before or simultaneous with op

before or simultaneous withop back to ToC or Object Property ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002081

Primitive instance level timing relation between events
has super-properties
temporally related to op
has sub-properties
before op, simultaneous with op

capable ofop back to ToC or Object Property ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002215

A relation between a material entity (such as a cell) and a process, in which the material entity has the ability to carry out the process.
Source
http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/20123131
http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/21208450
Example 
mechanosensory neuron capable of detection of mechanical stimulus involved in sensory perception (GO:0050974)
osteoclast SubClassOf 'capable of' some 'bone resorption'
has super-properties

capable of negatively regulatingop back to ToC or Object Property ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002597

Holds between c and p if and only if c is capable of some activity a, and a negatively regulates p.
Source
http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ro/docs/causal-relations
has super-properties
capable of regulating op

capable of part ofop back to ToC or Object Property ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002216

c stands in this relationship to p if and only if there exists some p' such that c is capable_of p', and p' is part_of p.
has super-properties
causal agent in process op, functionally related to op
has sub-properties
capable of op, contributes to op

capable of positively regulatingop back to ToC or Object Property ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002598

Holds between c and p if and only if c is capable of some activity a, and a positively regulates p.
Source
http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ro/docs/causal-relations
Example 
renin -> arteriolar smooth muscle contraction
has super-properties
capable of regulating op

capable of regulatingop back to ToC or Object Property ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002596

Holds between c and p if and only if c is capable of some activity a, and a regulates p.
Source
http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ro/docs/causal-relations
Example 
pyrethroid -> growth

causal agent in process op

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002500

A relationship between a material entity and a process where the material entity has some causal role that influences the process

http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ro/docs/causal-relations

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002595

A relationship that holds between a material entity and a process in which causality is involved, with either the material entity or some part of the material entity exerting some influence over the process, or the process influencing some aspect of the material entity.
Source
http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ro/docs/causal-relations

causally related to op
has sub-properties
causal agent in process op, has part structure that is capable of op

causal relation between processesop back to ToC or Object Property ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002501

p is causally related to q if and only if p or any part of p and q or any part of q are linked by a chain of events where each event pair is one where the execution of p influences the execution of q. p may be upstream, downstream, part of, or a container of q.
Source
http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ro/docs/causal-relations
has super-properties
causally related to op
has sub-properties
causally downstream of or within op, causally upstream of or within op

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002566

The entity or characteristic A is causally upstream of the entity or characteristic B, A having an effect on B. An entity corresponds to any biological type of entity as long as a mass is measurable. A characteristic corresponds to a particular specificity of an entity (e.g., phenotype, shape, size).
Source
http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ro/docs/causal-relations
has super-properties
causal relation between entities op
has sub-properties
determines op, regulates quantity of op

causally related toop back to ToC or Object Property ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002410

relation that links two events, processes, states, or objects such that one event, process, state, or object (a cause) contributes to the production of another event, process, state, or object (an effect) where the cause is partly or wholly responsible for the effect, and the effect is partly or wholly dependent on the cause.
has sub-properties
causal relation between entities op, causal relation between material entity and a process op, causal relation between processes op, process has causal agent op, realized in response to op, realized in response to stimulus op, regulates characteristic op, related via exposure to op

causally upstream ofop back to ToC or Object Property ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002411

p is causally upstream of q iff p is causally related to q, the end of p precedes the end of q, and p is not an occurrent part of q.

has characteristics: transitive

has super-properties
causally upstream of or within op, precedes op

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002418

p is 'causally upstream or within' q iff p is causally related to q, and the end of p precedes, or is coincident with, the end of q.

has characteristics: transitive

causal relation between processes op

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002305

p is causally upstream of, negative effect q iff p is casually upstream of q, and the execution of p decreases the execution of q.

causally upstream of op, causally upstream of or within, negative effect op
has sub-properties
negatively regulates op, removes input for op

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002304

p is causally upstream of, positive effect q iff p is casually upstream of q, and the execution of p is required for the execution of q.
holds between x and y if and only if x is causally upstream of y and the progression of x increases the frequency, rate or extent of y
has super-properties
causally upstream of op, causally upstream of or within, positive effect op
has sub-properties
constitutively upstream of op, positively regulates op

characteristic ofop back to ToC or Object Property ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000052

a relation between a specifically dependent continuant (the characteristic) and any other entity (the bearer), in which the characteristic depends on the bearer for its existence.
Note that this relation was previously called "inheres in", but was changed to be called "characteristic of" because BFO2 uses "inheres in" in a more restricted fashion. This relation differs from BFO2:inheres_in in two respects: (1) it does not impose a range constraint, and thus it allows qualities of processes, as well as of information entities, whereas BFO2 restricts inheres_in to only apply to independent continuants (2) it is declared functional, i.e. something can only be a characteristic of one thing.
Example 
this fragility is a characteristic of this vase
this red color is a characteristic of this apple

has characteristics: functional

has super-properties
characteristic of part of op
has sub-properties
disposition of op, function of op, quality of op, role of op
is inverse of
has characteristic op

characteristic of part ofop back to ToC or Object Property ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002314

q characteristic of part of w if and only if there exists some p such that q inheres in p and p part of w.
Source
http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/20064205
has super-properties
depends on op
has sub-properties
characteristic of op

coincident withop back to ToC or Object Property ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002008

A relation that holds between two linear structures that are approximately parallel to each other for their entire length and where either the two structures are adjacent to each other or one is part of the other.
Example: if we define region of chromosome as any subdivision of a chromosome along its long axis, then we can define a region of chromosome that contains only gene x as 'chromosome region' that coincident_with some 'gene x', where the term gene X corresponds to a genomic sequence.
has super-properties
mereotopologically related to op

colocalizes withop back to ToC or Object Property ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002325

a colocalizes_with b if and only if a is transiently or peripherally associated with b[GO].
Example 
Clp1p relocalizes from the nucleolus to the spindle and site of cell division; i.e. it is associated transiently with the spindle pole body and the contractile ring (evidence from GFP fusion). Clp1p colocalizes_with spindle pole body (GO:0005816) and contractile ring (GO:0005826)
has super-properties
mereotopologically related to op

composed primarily ofop back to ToC or Object Property ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002473

x composed_primarily_of y if and only if more than half of the mass of x is made from y or units of the same type as y.
Source
http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/22293552
Example 
'otolith organ' SubClassOf 'composed primarily of' some 'calcium carbonate'
has super-properties
has part op

concretizesop back to ToC or Object Property ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000059

b concretizes c =Def b is a process or a specifically dependent continuant & c is a generically dependent continuant & there is some time t such that c is the pattern or content which b shares at t with actual or potential copies
has sub-properties
concretizes at all times op
has domain
process c or specifically dependent continuant c
has range
generically dependent continuant c
is inverse of
is concretized by op

concretizesop back to ToC or Object Property ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000059

A relationship between a specifically dependent continuant and a generically dependent continuant, in which the generically dependent continuant depends on some independent continuant in virtue of the fact that the specifically dependent continuant also depends on that same independent continuant. Multiple specifically dependent continuants can concretize the same generically dependent continuant.
Example 
A journal article is an information artifact that inheres in some number of printed journals. For each copy of the printed journal there is some quality that carries the journal article, such as a pattern of ink. The quality (a specifically dependent continuant) concretizes the journal article (a generically dependent continuant), and both depend on that copy of the printed journal (an independent continuant).
An investigator reads a protocol and forms a plan to carry out an assay. The plan is a realizable entity (a specifically dependent continuant) that concretizes the protocol (a generically dependent continuant), and both depend on the investigator (an independent continuant). The plan is then realized by the assay (a process).

concretizes at all timesop back to ToC or Object Property ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000164

an s-dependent continuant b concretizes a g-dependent continuant c at all times =Def for all times t, b exists at t implies (c is the pattern or content which b shares at t with actual or potential copies)
has super-properties
concretizes op
has domain
process c or specifically dependent continuant c
has range
generically dependent continuant c

connected toop back to ToC or Object Property ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002170

a is connected to b if and only if a and b are discrete structure, and there exists some connecting structure c, such that c connects a and b
Source
http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/22293552
Example 
a 'toe distal phalanx bone' that is connected to a 'toe medial phalanx bone' (an interphalangeal joint *connects* these two bones).
has super-properties
mereotopologically related to op
has sub-properties
receives input from op, sends output to op

connectsop back to ToC or Object Property ToC

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002176

c connects a if and only if there exist some b such that a and b are similar parts of the same system, and c connects b, specifically, c connects a with b. When one structure connects two others it unites some aspect of the function or role they play within the system.
Source
http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/22293552
Example 
The M8 connects Glasgow and Edinburgh
a 'toe distal phalanx bone' that is connected to a 'toe medial phalanx bone' (an interphalangeal joint *connects* these two bones).
has super-properties
mereotopologically related to op

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0012009

p is constitutively upstream of q iff p is causally upstream of q, p is required for execution of q or a part of q, and the execution of p is approximately constant.
has super-properties
causally upstream of, positive effect op

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000176

b continuant part of c =Def b and c are continuants & there is some time t such that b and c exist at t & b continuant part of c at t
Example 
Milk teeth continuant part of human; surgically removed tumour continuant part of organism
has sub-properties
continuant part of at all times op, member part of op, proper continuant part of at some time op

IRI: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000177

b continuant part of c at all times =Def for all times t, (b exists at t, implies b continuant part of c at t & t is a temporal region & b and c are continuants)
Example 
Centre of mass of a material entity continuant part of material entity at all times; continuant fiat external boundary of an object continuant part of object at all times.

X continuous_with Y if and only if X and Y share a fiat boundary.
